Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- The executor handles step ordering, retry logic,\nparallel dispatch, and state management.\n\nNFR-007: No imports from superclaude.cli.sprint or superclaude.cli.roadmap.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport threading\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom typing import Callable, Protocol\n\nfrom .gates import gate_passed\nfrom .models import PipelineConfig, Step, StepResult, StepStatus\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.pipeline.executor\")\n\n\nclass StepRunner(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Callable that executes a single step's subprocess and returns a result.\n\n    The runner is responsible for:\n    - Launching the claude -p subprocess\n    - Waiting for completion (with timeout enforcement)\n    - Returning a StepResult with appropriate status\n\n    The runner is NOT responsible for:\n    - Retry logic (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    - Gate checking (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    - Step ordering (handled by execute_pipeline)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __call__(\n        self,\n        step: Step,\n        config: PipelineConfig,\n        cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n    ) -> StepResult: ...\n\n\ndef execute_pipeline(\n    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    run_step: StepRunner,\n    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\n    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\n    on_state_update: Callable[[dict], None] = lambda state: None,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool] = lambda: False,\n) -> list[StepResult]:\n    \"\"\"Generic pipeline executor.\n\n    Processes steps in order. Each element in ``steps`` is either:\n    - A single Step (executed sequentially)\n    - A list[Step] (all steps in the list executed in parallel)\n\n    For each step:\n    1. Call on_step_start(step)\n    2. Call run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\n    3. If step has gate criteria: run gate_passed()\n    4. If gate fails and attempts < retry_limit: retry (go to 2)\n    5. If gate fails and attempts exhausted: HALT\n    6. Call on_step_complete(step, result)\n    7. Call on_state_update(updated_state_dict)\n\n    For parallel step groups:\n    - All steps in the group run concurrently via _run_parallel_steps()\n    - If any step in the group fails, remaining steps are cancelled\n    - All results from the group must be PASS before proceeding\n\n    Returns list of all StepResults (one per step, flattened).\n    \"\"\"\n    all_results: list[StepResult] = []\n\n    for entry in steps:\n        if cancel_check():\n            break\n\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            # Parallel step group\n            for s in entry:\n                on_step_start(s)\n\n            group_results = _run_parallel_steps(entry, config, run_step, cancel_check)\n            all_results.extend(group_results)\n\n            for r in group_results:\n                on_step_complete(r.step, r)\n\n            on_state_update(_build_state(all_results))\n\n            # If any step in the group failed, halt\n            if any(r.status != StepStatus.PASS for r in group_results):\n                break\n        else:\n            # Sequential step\n            result = _execute_single_step(entry, config, run_step, cancel_check, on_step_start, on_step_complete)\n            all_results.append(result)\n            on_state_update(_build_state(all_results))\n\n            if result.status != StepStatus.PASS:\n                break\n\n    return all_results\n\n\ndef _execute_single_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    run_step: StepRunner,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n    on_step_start: Callable[[Step], None] = lambda s: None,\n    on_step_complete: Callable[[Step, StepResult], None] = lambda s, r: None,\n) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single step with retry logic and gate checking.\"\"\"\n    on_step_start(step)\n    max_attempts = step.retry_limit + 1  # retry_limit=1 means 2 attempts total\n\n    for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):\n        if cancel_check():\n            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            result = StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=attempt,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=now,\n                finished_at=now,\n            )\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        result = run_step(step, config, cancel_check)\n        result = StepResult(\n            step=result.step,\n            status=result.status,\n            attempt=attempt,\n            gate_failure_reason=result.gate_failure_reason,\n            started_at=result.started_at,\n            finished_at=result.finished_at,\n        )\n\n        # If step has no gate, trust the StepRunner's status\n        if step.gate is None:\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # If runner already reported failure/timeout, skip gate check\n        if result.status in (StepStatus.TIMEOUT, StepStatus.CANCELLED):\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # Run gate check\n        passed, reason = gate_passed(step.output_file, step.gate)\n        if passed:\n            result = StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.PASS,\n                attempt=attempt,\n                gate_failure_reason=None,\n                started_at=result.started_at,\n                finished_at=result.finished_at,\n            )\n            on_step_complete(step, result)\n            return result\n\n        # Gate failed\n        _log.info(\"Gate failed for step '%s' (attempt %d/%d): %s\", step.id, attempt, max_attempts, reason)\n\n        if attempt < max_attempts:\n            continue  # retry\n\n        # Exhausted retries\n        result = StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=attempt,\n            gate_failure_reason=reason,\n            started_at=result.started_at,\n            finished_at=result.finished_at,\n        )\n        on_step_complete(step, result)\n        return result\n\n    # Should not reach here, but safety fallback\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n        attempt=max_attempts,\n        gate_failure_reason=\"Unexpected: exhausted retry loop\",\n        started_at=now,\n        finished_at=now,\n    )\n\n\ndef _run_parallel_steps(\n    steps: list[Step],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    run_step: StepRunner,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> list[StepResult]:\n    \"\"\"Run steps concurrently. Returns when all complete or one fails.\n\n    Cross-cancellation: if any step fails after retry, a shared Event\n    is set, causing remaining steps to terminate their subprocesses.\n    \"\"\"\n    cancel_event = threading.Event()\n    results: list[StepResult | None] = [None] * len(steps)\n\n    def _worker(idx: int, step: Step) -> None:\n        def combined_cancel() -> bool:\n            return cancel_check() or cancel_event.is_set()\n\n        result = _execute_single_step(step, config, run_step, combined_cancel)\n        results[idx] = result\n        if result.status != StepStatus.PASS:\n            cancel_event.set()\n\n    threads = [\n        threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(i, s), daemon=True)\n        for i, s in enumerate(steps)\n    ]\n    for t in threads:\n        t.start()\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    # Replace None entries (shouldn't happen, but defensive)\n    final: list[StepResult] = []\n    for i, r in enumerate(results):\n        if r is not None:\n            final.append(r)\n        else:\n            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            final.append(StepResult(\n                step=steps[i],\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=0,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Thread did not produce a result\",\n                started_at=now,\n                finished_at=now,\n            ))\n\n    return final\n\n\ndef _build_state(results: list[StepResult]) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build a state dict from accumulated results.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"steps\": {\n            r.step.id: {\n                \"status\": r.status.value,\n                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n                \"gate_failure_reason\": r.gate_failure_reason,\n            }\n            for r in results\n        },\n        \"total\": len(results),\n        \"passed\": sum(1 for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS),\n        \"failed\": sum(1 for r in results if r.status.is_failure),\n    }\n","numLines":266,"startLine":1,"totalLines":266}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JoS2ZhyBhV4JgiJVmJ4Tr5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n     2→\n     3→Validates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n     4→  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n     5→  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n     6→  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n     7→  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n     8→\n     9→NFR-003: No subprocess import. NFR-007: No sprint/roadmap imports.\n    10→\"\"\"\n    11→\n    12→from __future__ import annotations\n    13→\n    14→from pathlib import Path\n    15→\n    16→from .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n    17→\n    18→\n    19→def gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    20→    \"\"\"Validate a step's output against its gate criteria.\n    21→\n    22→    Returns (True, None) on pass.\n    23→    Returns (False, reason) on failure where reason is human-readable.\n    24→    \"\"\"\n    25→    tier = criteria.enforcement_tier\n    26→\n    27→    # EXEMPT: always passes\n    28→    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n    29→        return True, None\n    30→\n    31→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    32→    if not output_file.exists():\n    33→        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n    34→\n    35→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    36→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    37→    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n    38→        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n    39→\n    40→    # LIGHT stops here\n    41→    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n    42→        return True, None\n    43→\n    44→    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    45→    lines = content.splitlines()\n    46→    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n    47→        return False, (\n    48→            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n    49→            f\"in {output_file}\"\n    50→        )\n    51→\n    52→    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    53→    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n    54→        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file)\n    55→        if not ok:\n    56→            return False, reason\n    57→\n    58→    # STANDARD stops here\n    59→    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n    60→        return True, None\n    61→\n    62→    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    63→    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n    64→        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n    65→            if not check.check_fn(content):\n    66→                return False, f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\"\n    67→\n    68→    return True, None\n    69→\n    70→\n    71→def _check_frontmatter(\n    72→    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n    73→) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    74→    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\"\"\"\n    75→    # Frontmatter is delimited by --- at start and end\n    76→    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    77→    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n    78→        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter missing or unparseable in {output_file}: no opening ---\"\n    79→\n    80→    # Find closing ---\n    81→    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    82→    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    83→    if end_idx == -1:\n    84→        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter missing or unparseable in {output_file}: no closing ---\"\n    85→\n    86→    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n    87→\n    88→    # Simple key extraction (key: value lines)\n    89→    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    90→    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n    91→        line = line.strip()\n    92→        if \":\" in line:\n    93→            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n    94→            if key:\n    95→                found_keys.add(key)\n    96→\n    97→    for field in required_fields:\n    98→        if field not in found_keys:\n    99→            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n   100→\n   101→    return True, None\n   102→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"69de7787-b266-4df7-b3e2-5105646aaa4f","uuid":"eed03232-feb7-4066-bec9-b1dc7c77478f","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py","content":"\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n\nValidates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n\nNFR-003: No subprocess import. NFR-007: No sprint/roadmap imports.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\ndef gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    \"\"\"Validate a step's output against its gate criteria.\n\n    Returns (True, None) on pass.\n    Returns (False, reason) on failure where reason is human-readable.\n    \"\"\"\n    tier = criteria.enforcement_tier\n\n    # EXEMPT: always passes\n    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    if not output_file.exists():\n        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n\n    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n\n    # LIGHT stops here\n    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n        return False, (\n            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n            f\"in {output_file}\"\n        )\n\n    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file)\n        if not ok:\n            return False, reason\n\n    # STANDARD stops here\n    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n            if not check.check_fn(content):\n                return False, f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\"\n\n    return True, None\n\n\ndef _check_frontmatter(\n    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\"\"\"\n    # Frontmatter is delimited by --- at start and end\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter missing or unparseable in {output_file}: no opening ---\"\n\n    # Find closing ---\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter missing or unparseable in {output_file}: no closing ---\"\n\n    frontmatter_text = rest[:end_idx]\n\n    # Simple key extraction (key: value lines)\n    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found_keys.add(key)\n\n    for field in required_fields:\n        if field not in found_keys:\n            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n\n    return True, None\n","numLines":102,"startLine":1,"totalLines":102}}}

via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n   435→    import os as _os\n   436→\n   437→    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   438→    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n   439→    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   440→    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n   441→\n   442→\n   443→def read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n   444→    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n   445→    if not path.exists():\n   446→        return None\n   447→    try:\n   448→        text = 453→        return None\n   454→\n   455→\n   456→def execute_roadmap(config: RoadmapConfig, resume: bool = False) -> None:\n   457→    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n   458→\n   459→    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n   460→    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n   461→    \"\"\"\n   462→    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True,     return\n   470→\n   471→    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n   472→    if resume:\n   473→        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n   474→\n   475→        steps = _save_state(config, results)\n   488→\n   489→    # Check for failures\n   490→    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   491→    if failures:\n   492→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n   493→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n   494→        sys.exit(1)\n   495→\n   496→    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete:  508→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   509→    state = read_state(state_file)\n   510→    force_extract = False\n   511→    if state is not None:\n   512→ 515→            print(\n   516→                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n   517→                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n   518→                f\" 535→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   536→            # Parallel group: check all steps\n   537→            all_pass = True\n   538→            for s in entry:\n   539→                if         break\n   544→                else:\n   545→                    all_pass = False\n   546→                    break\n   547→            if all_pass:\n   548→                skipped += CLAUDECODE= env prefix prevents nested\n    session detection. stdout/stderr redirected to files.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        prompt: str,\n        output_file: Path,\n        error_file: Path,\n        max_turns: int = 50,\n        model: str = \"\",\n        permission_flag: str = \"--dangerously-skip-permissions\",\n        timeout_seconds: self.output_format = output_format\n        self.extra_args = extra_args or []\n        self._process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None\n        self._stdout_fh = None\n        self._stderr_fh = None\n\n    def build_command(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Build the claude CLI command.\"\"\"\n        cmd = [\n            \"claude\",\n            \"--print\",\n            \"--verbose\",\n            self.permission_flag,\n            \"--no-session-persistence\",\n            \"--max-turns\",\n            str(self.max_turns),\n            \"--output-format\",\n return cmd\n\n    def build_env(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Build environment for the child process.\n\n        Remove CLAUDECODE and CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT to prevent\n        nested-session detection in the child claude process.\n        \"\"\"\n        env = os.environ.copy()\n        env.pop(\"CLAUDECODE\", None)\n        env.pop(\"CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT\", None)\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UdAkxv1XpvzyRu78iKn2Jm","type":"tool_result","content":"  1960→\n  1961→## Meta-Orchestrator: Pipeline Mode\n  1962→\n  1963→<!-- Meta-Orchestrator: Pipeline definition parsing, DAG construction, phase execution, artifact routing, and pipeline control.\n  1964→     This section is the routing target for step_0_pipeline_guard when --pipeline flag is detected.\n  1965→     Track A deliverables: D-0005 through D-0010 (M2), D-0020 through D-0027 (M4). -->\n  1966→\n  1967→When `--pipeline` is detected by `step_0_pipeline_guard`, execution routes here. The Meta-Orchestrator parses the pipeline definition (inline shorthand or YAML file), builds a DAG, validates it, and either renders a dry-run plan or executes phases sequentially/in-parallel.\n  1968→\n  1969→### Pipeline Definition Parsing\n  1970→\n  1971→Two input formats are supported. Both produce the same structured phase list output.\n  1972→\n  1973→#### Inline Shorthand Parser (D-0005)\n  1974→\n  1975→Parses `--pipeline \"<shorthand>\"` syntax into a structured phase list.\n  1976→\n  1977→```yaml\n  1978→inline_shorthand_parser:\n  1979→  grammar:\n  1980→    tokens:\n  1981→      ARROW: \"->\"           # Sequential dependency (left must complete before right)\n  1982→      PIPE: \"|\"             # Parallel grouping (phases at same dependency level)\n  1983→      GENERATE: \"generate:\" # Generate phase type, followed by agent spec list\n  1984→      COMPARE: \"compare\"    # Compare phase type (no agents required)\n  1985→      AGENT_LIST: \"<agent_spec>[,<agent_spec>,...]\"  # Comma-separated agent specs\n  1986→      PHASE_ID: \"[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*\"  # Alphanumeric identifier for a phase\n  1987→\n  1988→    syntax: |\n  1989→      pipeline     ::= phase_group ( '->' phase_group )*\n  1990→      phase_group  ::= phase ( '|' phase )*\n  1991→      phase        ::= PHASE_ID ':' phase_type\n  1992→                      | phase_type\n  1993→      phase_type   ::= 'generate:' AGENT_LIST\n  1994→                      | 'compare'\n  1995→\n  1996→    examples:\n  1997→      simple_3phase: |\n  1998→        generate:opus:architect,haiku:architect -> generate:opus:security,haiku:security -> compare\n  1999→        # Phase 1: generate with opus:architect and haiku:architect\n  2000→        # Phase 2: generate with opus:security and haiku:security (depends on Phase 1)\n  2001→        # Phase 3: compare (depends on Phase 2)\n  2002→\n  2003→      parallel_generate: |\n  2004→        generate:opus:architect,haiku:architect | generate:opus:security,haiku:security -> compare\n  2005→        # Phase 1a: generate with opus:architect,haiku:architect (parallel with 1b)\n  2006→        # Phase 1b: generate with opus:security,haiku:security (parallel with 1a)\n  2007→        # Phase 2: compare (depends on both 1a and 1b)\n  2008→\n  2009→      named_phases: |\n  2010→        arch:generate:opus:architect,haiku:architect -> sec:generate:opus:security -> final:compare\n  2011→        # Named phases for readability and depends_on references\n  2012→\n  2013→  tokenizer:\n  2014→    step_1: \"Split input on whitespace-padded '->' to get phase groups\"\n  2015→    step_2: \"Split each phase group on whitespace-padded '|' to get parallel phases\"\n  2016→    step_3: \"For each phase, extract optional name (before ':'), phase type, and agent list\"\n  2017→    step_4: \"Validate each token against grammar rules\"\n  2018→\n  2019→  parser_output:\n  2020→    format: \"Structured phase list (identical to YAML loader output)\"\n  2021→    schema:\n  2022→      phases:\n  2023→        - id: \"Auto-assigned (phase_1, phase_2, ...) or user-named\"\n  2024→          type: \"generate | compare\"\n  2025→          agents: [\"agent_spec_1\", \"agent_spec_2\"]  # Empty for compare\n  2026→          depends_on: [\"phase_id_1\", ...]            # Derived from '->' ordering\n  2027→          parallel_group: 0                           # Phases with same group run concurrently\n  2028→\n  2029→  error_handling:\n  2030→    missing_arrow: \"STOP: 'Malformed shorthand: expected -> between phase groups, got: <token>'\"\n  2031→    unmatched_pipe: \"STOP: 'Malformed shorthand: unmatched | in phase group: <group>'\"\n  2032→    unknown_type: \"STOP: 'Unknown phase type: <type>. Expected generate:<agents> or compare'\"\n  2033→    empty_agents: \"STOP: 'generate phase requires at least one agent: generate:<agent_spec>'\"\n  2034→    duplicate_name: \"STOP: 'Duplicate phase name: <name>. Each phase must have a unique identifier'\"\n  2035→```\n  2036→\n  2037→#### YAML Pipeline File Loader (D-0006)\n  2038→\n  2039→Parses `--pipeline @path.yaml` into the same structured phase list.\n  2040→\n  2041→```yaml\n  2042→yaml_pipeline_loader:\n  2043→  trigger: \"--pipeline value starts with '@'\"\n  2044→  file_resolution: \"Strip leading '@', resolve path relative to working directory\"\n  2045→\n  2046→  schema:\n  2047→    required_top_level:\n  2048→      phases: \"Array of phase definitions (required)\"\n  2049→\n  2050→    per_phase_fields:\n  2051→      required:\n  2052→        type: \"string — 'generate' or 'compare'\"\n  2053→      optional:\n  2054→        id: \"string — unique phase identifier (auto-assigned if absent)\"\n  2055→        agents: \"array of strings — agent specs (required for generate, forbidden for compare)\"\n  2056→        depends_on: \"array of strings — phase IDs this phase depends on\"\n  2057→        config: \"object — phase-specific configuration overrides\"\n  2058→\n  2059→    config_fields:\n  2060→      depth: \"string — quick/standard/deep (overrides global --depth for this phase)\"\n  2061→      convergence: \"number — 0.50-0.99 (overrides global --convergence)\"\n  2062→      output: \"string — output path override for this phase\"\n  2063→\n  2064→  schema_validation:\n  2065→    unknown_fields: \"STOP: 'Unknown field in phase definition: <field>. Allowed: id, type, agents, depends_on, config'\"\n  2066→    missing_required: \"STOP: 'Missing required field in phase definition: type'\"\n  2067→    type_invalid: \"STOP: 'Invalid phase type: <value>. Must be generate or compare'\"\n  2068→    agents_on_compare: \"STOP: 'compare phase must not specify agents'\"\n  2069→    no_agents_on_generate: \"STOP: 'generate phase requires agents field with at least one agent spec'\"\n  2070→    empty_phases: \"STOP: 'Pipeline must contain at least one phase'\"\n  2071→\n  2072→  example_yaml: |\n  2073→    # 3-phase canonical pipeline\n  2074→    phases:\n  2075→      - id: arch_gen\n  2076→        type: generate\n  2077→        agents: [\"opus:architect\", \"haiku:architect\"]\n  2078→      - id: sec_gen\n  2079→        type: generate\n  2080→        agents: [\"opus:security\", \"haiku:security\"]\n  2081→        depends_on: [\"arch_gen\"]\n  2082→      - id: final_compare\n  2083→        type: compare\n  2084→        depends_on: [\"sec_gen\"]\n  2085→\n  2086→  output: \"Structured phase list (identical schema to inline parser output)\"\n  2087→```\n  2088→\n  2089→### DAG Builder (D-0007)\n  2090→\n  2091→Constructs a directed acyclic graph from the structured phase list, identifying parallel phase groups and sequential gates.\n  2092→\n  2093→```yaml\n  2094→dag_builder:\n  2095→  input: \"Structured phase list from inline parser or YAML loader\"\n  2096→\n  2097→  construction:\n  2098→    step_1_create_nodes:\n  2099→      action: \"Create one node per phase definition\"\n  2100→      node_schema:\n  2101→        id: \"Phase ID (auto-assigned or user-named)\"\n  2102→        type: \"generate | compare\"\n  2103→        agents: \"Agent spec list (empty for compare)\"\n  2104→        config: \"Phase-specific configuration overrides\"\n  2105→        level: \"Dependency level (assigned in step 3)\"\n  2106→\n  2107→    step_2_create_edges:\n  2108→      action: \"Create directed edges from depends_on references\"\n  2109→      edge_schema:\n  2110→        from: \"Dependency phase ID (must complete first)\"\n  2111→        to: \"Dependent phase ID (waits for from)\"\n  2112→      implicit_edges: |\n  2113→        If no explicit depends_on and phases are separated by '->':\n  2114→        each phase group depends on the previous group.\n  2115→\n  2116→    step_3_assign_levels:\n  2117→      action: \"Topological sort to assign dependency levels\"\n  2118→      algorithm: \"Kahn's algorithm (BFS-based topological sort)\"\n  2119→      level_assignment: |\n  2120→        Phases with no dependencies: level 0\n  2121→        Phases depending only on level-N phases: level N+1\n  2122→      parallelization: \"Phases at the same level can execute concurrently\"\n  2123→\n  2124→  validation:\n  2125→    cycle_detection: \"See Cycle Detection section below\"\n  2126→    reference_integrity: \"See Reference Integrity Validation section below\"\n  2127→\n  2128→  output_schema:\n  2129→    nodes: \"Array of phase nodes with assigned levels\"\n  2130→    edges: \"Array of directed edges (from -> to)\"\n  2131→    levels: \"Array of arrays — phases grouped by dependency level\"\n  2132→    execution_order: \"Flattened topological order for sequential execution\"\n  2133→\n  2134→  serialization:\n  2135→    format: \"YAML-compatible structure for dry-run output and manifest consumption\"\n  2136→    example: |\n  2137→      dag:\n  2138→        nodes:\n  2139→          - {id: arch_gen, type: generate, level: 0}\n  2140→          - {id: sec_gen, type: generate, level: 1}\n  2141→          - {id: final_compare, type: compare, level: 2}\n  2142→        edges:\n  2143→          - {from: arch_gen, to: sec_gen}\n  2144→          - {from: sec_gen, to: final_compare}\n  2145→        levels:\n  2146→          - [arch_gen]\n  2147→          - [sec_gen]\n  2148→          - [final_compare]\n  2149→```\n  2150→\n  2151→### Cycle Detection (D-0008)\n  2152→\n  2153→Detects circular dependencies during DAG construction and reports the exact cycle path.\n  2154→\n  2155→```yaml\n  2156→cycle_detection:\n  2157→  integration_point: \"Runs during dag_builder.step_3_assign_levels, before level assignment\"\n  2158→  algorithm: \"DFS-based cycle detection with path tracking\"\n  2159→\n  2160→  implementation:\n  2161→    step_1: \"Initialize all nodes as UNVISITED\"\n  2162→    step_2: \"For each UNVISITED node, start DFS traversal\"\n  2163→    step_3: \"Mark entering node as IN_PROGRESS\"\n  2164→    step_4: \"For each neighbor (depends_on target), check state:\"\n  2165→    step_4a: \"  IN_PROGRESS → cycle detected, extract cycle path\"\n  2166→    step_4b: \"  UNVISITED → recurse\"\n  2167→    step_4c: \"  VISITED → skip (already processed)\"\n  2168→    step_5: \"Mark exiting node as VISITED\"\n  2169→\n  2170→  error_reporting:\n  2171→    format: \"Circular dependency detected: {path}\"\n  2172→    example_short: \"Circular dependency detected: A -> B -> A\"\n  2173→    example_long: \"Circular dependency detected: A -> B -> C -> A\"\n  2174→    action: \"STOP pipeline execution immediately — no phases are executed\"\n  2175→\n  2176→  timing: \"Must complete before any phase execution begins\"\n  2177→```\n  2178→\n  2179→### Reference Integrity Validation (D-0009)\n  2180→\n  2181→Validates that all `depends_on` phase IDs reference existing phases in the phase list.\n  2182→\n  2183→```yaml\n  2184→reference_integrity:\n  2185→  integration_point: \"Runs during dag_builder.step_2_create_edges, before cycle detection\"\n  2186→  algorithm: \"Collect all phase IDs into a set, then check each depends_on reference\"\n  2187→\n  2188→  implementation:\n  2189→    step_1: \"Build set of all defined phase IDs from structured phase list\"\n  2190→    step_2: \"For each phase, check each depends_on entry against the ID set\"\n  2191→    step_3: \"Collect ALL invalid references (do not fail-fast on first)\"\n  2192→    step_4: \"If any invalid references found, report all in a single error\"\n  2193→\n  2194→  error_reporting:\n  2195→    single: \"Unknown phase reference: <id>\"\n  2196→    multiple: \"Unknown phase references: <id1>, <id2>\"\n  2197→    action: \"STOP pipeline execution — report all invalid references before stopping\"\n  2198→\n  2199→  valid_pass: \"Phases with correct depends_on references pass without error output\"\n  2200→```\n  2201→\n  2202→### Dry-Run Render (D-0010)\n  2203→\n  2204→Validates the DAG and outputs the execution plan without executing any phases.\n  2205→\n  2206→```yaml\n  2207→dry_run:\n  2208→  trigger: \"--dry-run flag present (in combination with --pipeline)\"\n  2209→  alias: \"--pipeline-dry-run\"\n  2210→\n  2211→  implementation:\n  2212→    step_1: \"Parse pipeline definition (inline or YAML)\"\n  2213→    step_2: \"Build DAG (includes cycle detection and reference integrity)\"\n  2214→    step_3: \"Render execution plan to output\"\n  2215→    step_4: \"Exit without executing any phases\"\n  2216→\n  2217→  output_format:\n  2218→    sections:\n  2219→      pipeline_summary:\n  2220→        content: |\n  2221→          # Pipeline Execution Plan (Dry Run)\n  2222→          - Total phases: <N>\n  2223→          - Parallel groups: <N>\n  2224→          - Estimated execution levels: <N>\n  2225→\n  2226→      phase_table:\n  2227→        columns: [\"Phase ID\", \"Type\", \"Agents\", \"Depends On\", \"Level\", \"Parallel Group\"]\n  2228→        content: \"One row per phase from DAG nodes\"\n  2229→\n  2230→      execution_order:\n  2231→        content: |\n  2232→          ## Execution Order\n  2233→          Level 0: <phase_ids> (parallel)\n  2234→          Level 1: <phase_ids> (parallel)\n  2235→          ...\n  2236→\n  2237→      estimated_costs:\n  2238→        content: |\n  2239→          ## Estimated Token Costs\n  2240→          - generate phases: ~<N> tokens each (based on agent count and depth)\n  2241→          - compare phases: ~<N> tokens each (based on variant count)\n  2242→          - Total estimated: ~<N> tokens\n  2243→\n  2244→  output_routing:\n  2245→    default: \"Console (stdout)\"\n  2246→    file: \"--output <path> writes to file instead of console\"\n  2247→\n  2248→  validation_guarantee: |\n  2249→    If dry-run completes without error, the pipeline definition is valid:\n  2250→    - All phase types are recognized\n  2251→    - All depends_on references are valid\n  2252→    - No circular dependencies exist\n  2253→    - DAG can be constructed and topologically sorted\n  2254→```\n  2255→\n  2256→<!-- Phase Execution Engine (M4) will extend this section with:\n  2257→     - Phase Executor (D-0020): translates phase config v1.0.0*"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"69de7787-b266-4df7-b3e2-5105646aaa4f","uuid":"001ca860-10cf-41f2-8df3-944f6970d9c5","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-adversarial-protocol/SKILL.md","content":"\n## Meta-Orchestrator: Pipeline Mode\n\n<!-- Meta-Orchestrator: Pipeline definition parsing, DAG construction, phase execution, artifact routing, and pipeline control.\n     This section is the routing target for step_0_pipeline_guard when --pipeline flag is detected.\n     Track A deliverables: D-0005 through D-0010 (M2), D-0020 through D-0027 (M4). -->\n\nWhen `--pipeline` is detected by `step_0_pipeline_guard`, execution routes here. The Meta-Orchestrator parses the pipeline definition (inline shorthand or YAML file), builds a DAG, validates it, and either renders a dry-run plan or executes phases sequentially/in-parallel.\n\n### Pipeline Definition Parsing\n\nTwo input formats are supported. Both produce the same structured phase list output.\n\n#### Inline Shorthand Parser (D-0005)\n\nParses `--pipeline \"<shorthand>\"` syntax into a structured phase list.\n\n```yaml\ninline_shorthand_parser:\n  grammar:\n    tokens:\n      ARROW: \"->\"           # Sequential dependency (left must complete before right)\n      PIPE: \"|\"             # Parallel grouping (phases at same dependency level)\n      GENERATE: \"generate:\" # Generate phase type, followed by agent spec list\n      COMPARE: \"compare\"    # Compare phase type (no agents required)\n      AGENT_LIST: \"<agent_spec>[,<agent_spec>,...]\"  # Comma-separated agent specs\n      PHASE_ID: \"[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*\"  # Alphanumeric identifier for a phase\n\n    syntax: |\n      pipeline     ::= phase_group ( '->' phase_group )*\n      phase_group  ::= phase ( '|' phase )*\n      phase        ::= PHASE_ID ':' phase_type\n                      | phase_type\n      phase_type   ::= 'generate:' AGENT_LIST\n                      | 'compare'\n\n    examples:\n      simple_3phase: |\n        generate:opus:architect,haiku:architect -> generate:opus:security,haiku:security -> compare\n        # Phase 1: generate with opus:architect and haiku:architect\n        # Phase 2: generate with opus:security and haiku:security (depends on Phase 1)\n        # Phase 3: compare (depends on Phase 2)\n\n      parallel_generate: |\n        generate:opus:architect,haiku:architect | generate:opus:security,haiku:security -> compare\n        # Phase 1a: generate with opus:architect,haiku:architect (parallel with 1b)\n        # Phase 1b: generate with opus:security,haiku:security (parallel with 1a)\n        # Phase 2: compare (depends on both 1a and 1b)\n\n      named_phases: |\n        arch:generate:opus:architect,haiku:architect -> sec:generate:opus:security -> final:compare\n        # Named phases for readability and depends_on references\n\n  tokenizer:\n    step_1: \"Split input on whitespace-padded '->' to get phase groups\"\n    step_2: \"Split each phase group on whitespace-padded '|' to get parallel phases\"\n    step_3: \"For each phase, extract optional name (before ':'), phase type, and agent list\"\n    step_4: \"Validate each token against grammar rules\"\n\n  parser_output:\n    format: \"Structured phase list (identical to YAML loader output)\"\n    schema:\n      phases:\n        - id: \"Auto-assigned (phase_1, phase_2, ...) or user-named\"\n          type: \"generate | compare\"\n          agents: [\"agent_spec_1\", \"agent_spec_2\"]  # Empty for compare\n          depends_on: [\"phase_id_1\", ...]            # Derived from '->' ordering\n          parallel_group: 0                           # Phases with same group run concurrently\n\n  error_handling:\n    missing_arrow: \"STOP: 'Malformed shorthand: expected -> between phase groups, got: <token>'\"\n    unmatched_pipe: \"STOP: 'Malformed shorthand: unmatched | in phase group: <group>'\"\n    unknown_type: \"STOP: 'Unknown phase type: <type>. Expected generate:<agents> or compare'\"\n    empty_agents: \"STOP: 'generate phase requires at least one agent: generate:<agent_spec>'\"\n    duplicate_name: \"STOP: 'Duplicate phase name: <name>. Each phase must have a unique identifier'\"\n```\n\n#### YAML Pipeline File Loader (D-0006)\n\nParses `--pipeline @path.yaml` into the same structured phase list.\n\n```yaml\nyaml_pipeline_loader:\n  trigger: \"--pipeline value starts with '@'\"\n  file_resolution: \"Strip leading '@', resolve path relative to working directory\"\n\n  schema:\n    required_top_level:\n      phases: \"Array of phase definitions (required)\"\n\n    per_phase_fields:\n      required:\n        type: \"string — 'generate' or 'compare'\"\n      optional:\n        id: \"string — unique phase identifier (auto-assigned if absent)\"\n        agents: \"array of strings — agent specs (required for generate, forbidden for compare)\"\n        depends_on: \"array of strings — phase IDs this phase depends on\"\n        config: \"object — phase-specific configuration overrides\"\n\n    config_fields:\n      depth: \"string — quick/standard/deep (overrides global --depth for this phase)\"\n      convergence: \"number — 0.50-0.99 (overrides global --convergence)\"\n      output: \"string — output path override for this phase\"\n\n  schema_validation:\n    unknown_fields: \"STOP: 'Unknown field in phase definition: <field>. Allowed: id, type, agents, depends_on, config'\"\n    missing_required: \"STOP: 'Missing required field in phase definition: type'\"\n    type_invalid: \"STOP: 'Invalid phase type: <value>. Must be generate or compare'\"\n    agents_on_compare: \"STOP: 'compare phase must not specify agents'\"\n    no_agents_on_generate: \"STOP: 'generate phase requires agents field with at least one agent spec'\"\n    empty_phases: \"STOP: 'Pipeline must contain at least one phase'\"\n\n  example_yaml: |\n    # 3-phase canonical pipeline\n    phases:\n      - id: arch_gen\n        type: generate\n        agents: [\"opus:architect\", \"haiku:architect\"]\n      - id: sec_gen\n        type: generate\n        agents: [\"opus:security\", \"haiku:security\"]\n        depends_on: [\"arch_gen\"]\n      - id: final_compare\n        type: compare\n        depends_on: [\"sec_gen\"]\n\n  output: \"Structured phase list (identical schema to inline parser output)\"\n```\n\n### DAG Builder (D-0007)\n\nConstructs a directed acyclic graph from the structured phase list, identifying parallel phase groups and sequential gates.\n\n```yaml\ndag_builder:\n  input: \"Structured phase list from inline parser or YAML loader\"\n\n  construction:\n    step_1_create_nodes:\n      action: \"Create one node per phase definition\"\n      node_schema:\n        id: \"Phase ID (auto-assigned or user-named)\"\n        type: \"generate | compare\"\n        agents: \"Agent spec list (empty for compare)\"\n        config: \"Phase-specific configuration overrides\"\n        level: \"Dependency level (assigned in step 3)\"\n\n    step_2_create_edges:\n      action: \"Create directed edges from depends_on references\"\n      edge_schema:\n        from: \"Dependency phase ID (must complete first)\"\n        to: \"Dependent phase ID (waits for from)\"\n      implicit_edges: |\n        If no explicit depends_on and phases are separated by '->':\n        each phase group depends on the previous group.\n\n    step_3_assign_levels:\n      action: \"Topological sort to assign dependency levels\"\n      algorithm: \"Kahn's algorithm (BFS-based topological sort)\"\n      level_assignment: |\n        Phases with no dependencies: level 0\n        Phases depending only on level-N phases: level N+1\n      parallelization: \"Phases at the same level can execute concurrently\"\n\n  validation:\n    cycle_detection: \"See Cycle Detection section below\"\n    reference_integrity: \"See Reference Integrity Validation section below\"\n\n  output_schema:\n    nodes: \"Array of phase nodes with assigned levels\"\n    edges: \"Array of directed edges (from -> to)\"\n    levels: \"Array of arrays — phases grouped by dependency level\"\n    execution_order: \"Flattened topological order for sequential execution\"\n\n  serialization:\n    format: \"YAML-compatible structure for dry-run output and manifest consumption\"\n    example: |\n      dag:\n        nodes:\n          - {id: arch_gen, type: generate, level: 0}\n          - {id: sec_gen, type: generate, level: 1}\n          - {id: final_compare, type: compare, level: 2}\n        edges:\n          - {from: arch_gen, to: sec_gen}\n          - {from: sec_gen, to: final_compare}\n        levels:\n          - [arch_gen]\n          - [sec_gen]\n          - [final_compare]\n```\n\n### Cycle Detection (D-0008)\n\nDetects circular dependencies during DAG construction and reports the exact cycle path.\n\n```yaml\ncycle_detection:\n  integration_point: \"Runs during dag_builder.step_3_assign_levels, before level assignment\"\n  algorithm: \"DFS-based cycle detection with path tracking\"\n\n  implementation:\n    step_1: \"Initialize all nodes as UNVISITED\"\n    step_2: \"For each UNVISITED node, start DFS traversal\"\n    step_3: \"Mark entering node as IN_PROGRESS\"\n    step_4: \"For each neighbor (depends_on target), check state:\"\n    step_4a: \"  IN_PROGRESS → cycle detected, extract cycle path\"\n    step_4b: \"  UNVISITED → recurse\"\n    step_4c: \"  VISITED → skip (already processed)\"\n    step_5: \"Mark exiting node as VISITED\"\n\n  error_reporting:\n    format: \"Circular dependency detected: {path}\"\n    example_short: \"Circular dependency detected: A -> B -> A\"\n    example_long: \"Circular dependency detected: A -> B -> C -> A\"\n    action: \"STOP pipeline execution immediately — no phases are executed\"\n\n  timing: \"Must complete before any phase execution begins\"\n```\n\n### Reference Integrity Validation (D-0009)\n\nValidates that all `depends_on` phase IDs reference existing phases in the phase list.\n\n```yaml\nreference_integrity:\n  integration_point: \"Runs during dag_builder.step_2_create_edges, before cycle detection\"\n  algorithm: \"Collect all phase IDs into a set, then check each depends_on reference\"\n\n  implementation:\n    step_1: \"Build set of all defined phase IDs from structured phase list\"\n    step_2: \"For each phase, check each depends_on entry against the ID set\"\n    step_3: \"Collect ALL invalid references (do not fail-fast on first)\"\n    step_4: \"If any invalid references found, report all in a single error\"\n\n  error_reporting:\n    single: \"Unknown phase reference: <id>\"\n    multiple: \"Unknown phase references: <id1>, <id2>\"\n    action: \"STOP pipeline execution — report all invalid references before stopping\"\n\n  valid_pass: \"Phases with correct depends_on references pass without error output\"\n```\n\n### Dry-Run Render (D-0010)\n\nValidates the DAG and outputs the execution plan without executing any phases.\n\n```yaml\ndry_run:\n  trigger: \"--dry-run flag present (in combination with --pipeline)\"\n  alias: \"--pipeline-dry-run\"\n\n  implementation:\n    step_1: \"Parse pipeline definition (inline or YAML)\"\n    step_2: \"Build DAG (includes cycle detection and reference integrity)\"\n    step_3: \"Render execution plan to output\"\n    step_4: \"Exit without executing any phases\"\n\n  output_format:\n    sections:\n      pipeline_summary:\n        content: |\n          # Pipeline Execution Plan (Dry Run)\n          - Total phases: <N>\n          - Parallel groups: <N>\n          - Estimated execution levels: <N>\n\n      phase_table:\n        columns: [\"Phase ID\", \"Type\", \"Agents\", \"Depends On\", \"Level\", \"Parallel Group\"]\n        content: \"One row per phase from DAG nodes\"\n\n      execution_order:\n        content: |\n          ## Execution Order\n          Level 0: <phase_ids> (parallel)\n          Level 1: <phase_ids> (parallel)\n          ...\n\n      estimated_costs:\n        content: |\n          ## Estimated Token Costs\n          - generate phases: ~<N> tokens each (based on agent count and depth)\n          - compare phases: ~<N> tokens each (based on variant count)\n          - Total estimated: ~<N> tokens\n\n  output_routing:\n    default: \"Console (stdout)\"\n    file: \"--output <path> writes to file instead of console\"\n\n  validation_guarantee: |\n    If dry-run completes without error, the pipeline definition is valid:\n    - All phase types are recognized\n    - All depends_on references are valid\n    - No circular dependencies exist\n    - DAG can be constructed and topologically sorted\n```\n\n<!-- Phase Execution Engine (M4) will extend this section with:\n Accepts multiple artifacts (files or generated variants), identifies differences and contradictions, orchestrates structured debate between agents, selects the strongest base via hybrid scoring, produces a refactoring plan, and executes it to produce a unified output.\n    19→\n    20→**Core Objective**: Verify and validate accuracy of statements in generated artifacts, weeding out hallucinations and sycophantic agreement through structured adversarial pressure using steelman debate strategy.\n    21→\n    22→**Key Differentiator**: Uses multi-model adversarial reasoning (10-15% accuracy gains, 30%+ factual error reduction) as a generic framework tool invocable by any SuperClaude command.\n    23→\n    24→## Required Input\n    25→\n    26→**MANDATORY**: Either `--compare` (Mode A), `--source`+`--generate`+`--agents` (Mode B), or `--pipeline` (Pipeline Mode) must be provided.\n    27→\n    28→```\n    29→/sc:adversarial --compare file1.md,file2.md[,...,fileN.md] [options]\n    30→/sc:adversarial --source <file> --generate <type> --agents <spec>[,...] [options]\n    31→/sc:adversarial --pipeline \"<shorthand>\" [options]\n    32→/sc:adversarial --pipeline @pipeline.yaml [options]\n    33→```\n    34→\n    35→## Triggers\n    36→\n    37→- Explicit: `/sc:adversarial --compare ...` or `/sc:adversarial --source ...`\n    38→- Keywords: \"adversarial debate\", \"compare variants\", \"merge best of\"\n    39→- Integration: Called by `/sc:roadmap` in multi-spec/multi-roadmap modes\n    40→\n    41→## Dual Input Modes\n    42→\n    43→### Mode A: Compare Existing Files (FR-001)\n    44→\n    45→```bash\n    46→/sc:adversarial --compare file1.md,file2.md[,...,file10.md]\n    47→```\n    48→\n    49→- Accepts 2-10 existing files for comparison, debate, and merge\n    50→- Files are copied to `<output>/adversarial/variant-N-original.md`\n    51→- Validation: All files must exist and be readable; 2-10 file count enforced\n    52→\n    53→### Mode B: Generate + Compare from Source (FR-001)\n    54→\n    55→```bash\n    56→/sc:adversarial --source source.md --generate <type> --agents <agent-spec>[,...]\n    57→```\n    58→\n    59→- Generates variant artifacts from source using specified agents in parallel\n    60→- Agent specification format: `<model>[:persona[:\"instruction\"]]`\n    61→  - `opus` — model only\n    62→  - `opus:architect` — model + persona\n    63→  - `opus:architect:\"focus on scalability\"` — model + persona + instruction\n    64→- Supported models: Any available (opus, sonnet, haiku, configured aliases)\n    65→- Agent count: 2-10 (minimum 2 required for adversarial comparison)\n    66→- Variants written to `<output>/adversarial/variant-N-<model>-<persona>.md`\n    67→\n    68→## 5-Step Adversarial Protocol (FR-002)\n    69→\n    70→The core pipeline executes 5 sequential steps. Each step produces a documented artifact.\n    71→\n    72→### Step 1: Diff Analysis\n    73→\n    74→**Input**: All variant artifacts\n    75→**Delegation**: Analytical agent (or /sc:analyze equivalent)\n    76→**Process**:\n    77→\n    78→```yaml\n    79→diff_analysis:\n    80→  structural_diff:\n    81→    action: \"Compare section ordering, hierarchy depth, heading structure across variants\"\n    82→    output: \"Structural differences with severity ratings (Low/Medium/High)\"\n    83→\n    84→  content_diff:\n    85→    action: \"Compare approaches topic-by-topic, identify coverage differences\"\n    86→    output: \"Content differences with approach summaries per variant\"\n    87→\n    88→  contradiction_detection:\n    89→    action: \"Structured scan per contradiction detection protocol\"\n    90→    categories:\n    91→      - \"Opposing claims about the same subject\"\n    92→      - \"Requirement-constraint conflicts\"\n    93→      - \"Impossible timeline/dependency sequences\"\n    94→    rule: \"Claims must be specific enough to be falsifiable\"\n    95→\n    96→  unique_contribution_extraction:\n    97→    action: \"Identify ideas present in only one variant\"\n    98→    output: \"Unique contributions with value assessment (Low/Medium/High)\"\n    99→\n   100→  shared_assumption_extraction:\n   101→    action: \"Identify agreement points across variants and extract implicit shared assumptions (AD-2)\"\n   102→    process:\n   103→      - \"Scan all diff categories for agreement points (where all variants converge)\"\n   104→      - \"For each agreement point, enumerate underlying preconditions\"\n   105→      - \"Classify each precondition as STATED/UNSTATED/CONTRADICTED\"\n   106→    output: \"Shared assumptions with classification and A-NNN identifiers\"\n   107→    promotion: \"UNSTATED preconditions become synthetic [SHARED-ASSUMPTION] diff points\"\n   108→```\n   109→\n   110→**Output**: `adversarial/diff-analysis.md` — organized by category with severity ratings, plus Shared Assumptions section\n   111→**Template**: See `refs/artifact-templates.md` Section 1\n   112→\n   113→### Step 2: Adversarial Debate\n   114→\n   115→**Input**: All variants + diff-analysis.md\n   116→**Delegation**: debate-orchestrator agent coordinates; advocate agents participate\n   117→**Process**:\n   118→\n   119→```yaml\n   120→adversarial_debate:\n   121→  debate_topic_taxonomy:\n   122→    purpose: \"Three-level taxonomy (AD-5) ensuring state-mechanics-level debate cannot be bypassed\"\n   123→    levels:\n   124→      L1_surface:\n   125→        description: \"Surface-level differences: naming, formatting, style, wording choices\"\n   126→        auto_tag_signals:\n   127→          - organization, API design, component boundaries\"\n   137→        auto_tag_signals:\n   138→          - \"architecture\"\n   139→          - \"API design\"\n   140→          - \"component\"\n   141→          - \"module\"\n   142→          - \"interface\"\n   143→          - \"dependency\"\n   144→          - \"organization\"\n   145→        examples: \"Module decomposition, API endpoint design, data flow patterns\"\n   146→\n   147→      L3_state_mechanics:\n   148→        description: \"State-mechanics-level: state management, guard conditions, boundary validation, concurrency\"\n   149→        auto_tag_signals:\n   150→          - \"state\"\n   151→          - \"guard\"\n   152→          - \"boundary\"\n   153→          - \"invariant\"\n   154→          - \"concurrency\"\n   155→          - \"race condition\"\n   156→          - \"validation rule\"\n   157→          - \"transition\"\n   158→        examples: \"State machine transitions, input validation guards, concurrency controls\"\n   159→\n   160→    auto_tagging:\n   161→      action: \"Scan each diff point text for level-specific signal terms\"\n   162→      priority: \"L3 > L2 > L1 (if signals match multiple levels, assign highest)\"\n   163→      shared_assumption_rule: \"A-NNN points containing state/guard/boundary terms auto-tag as L3 (AC-AD5-3)\"\n   164→      fallback: \"If no signals match, assign L2 (structural) as default\"\n   165→      mutual_exclusivity: \"Each diff point receives exactly one taxonomy level\"\n   166→\n   167→  advocate_instantiation:\n   168→    action: \"Parse model[:persona[:\\\"instruction\\\"]] spec for each variant\"\n   169→    creation: \"Task agents with advocate prompts including variant + all others + diff-analysis\"\n   170→\n   171→  round_1_parallel:\n   172→    action: \"Each advocate presents strengths and critiques others\"\n   173→    execution: \"Parallel Task calls — all advocates run simultaneously\"\n   174→    steelman_requirement: \"Advocates MUST construct strongest version of opposing positions before critiquing\"\n   175→\n   176→  round_2_sequential:\n   177→    condition: \"--depth standard OR --depth deep\"\n   178→    action: \"Rebuttals — each advocate addresses criticisms from Round 1\"\n   179→    execution: \"Sequential — each receives all Round 1 transcripts\"\n   180→\n   181→  round_3_conditional:\n   182→    condition: \"--depth deep AND convergence < threshold\"\n   183→    action: \"Final arguments after considering all rebuttals\"\n   184→\n   185→  convergence_detection:\n   186→    metric: \"Percentage of diff points where agents agree on superior approach\"\n   187→    threshold: \"Configurable via --convergence (default 0.80)\"\n   188→    tracking: \"Per-point agreement tracking across rounds\"\n   189→\n   190→  scoring_matrix:\n   191→    action: \"For each diff point, record winner, confidence, evidence summary\"\n   192→    output: \"Per-point scoring matrix table\"\n   193→```\n   194→\n   195→**Output**: `adversarial/debate-transcript.md` — full debate with per-point scoring\n   196→**Template**: See
