{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019HAacyPKW4AXXBM46aJAag","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→---\n     2→name: sc:adversarial-protocol\n     3→description: Structured adversarial debate, comparison, and merge pipeline for 2-10 artifacts\n     4→allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Edit, Write, Bash, TodoWrite, Task, Skill\n     5→---\n     6→\n     7→# /sc:adversarial - Adversarial Debate & Merge Pipeline\n     8→\n     9→<!-- Extended metadata (for documentation, not parsed):\n    10→category: analysis\n    11→complexity: advanced\n    12→mcp-servers: [sequential, context7, serena]\n    13→personas: [architect, analyzer, scribe]\n    14→-->\n    15→\n    16→## Purpose\n    17→\n    18→Generic, reusable command implementing a structured adversarial debate, comparison, and merge pipeline. Accepts multiple artifacts (files or generated variants), identifies differences and contradictions, orchestrates structured debate between agents, selects the strongest base via hybrid scoring, produces a refactoring plan, and executes it to produce a unified output.\n    19→\n    20→**Core Objective**: Verify and validate accuracy of statements in generated artifacts, weeding out hallucinations and sycophantic agreement through structured adversarial pressure using steelman debate strategy.\n    21→\n    22→**Key Differentiator**: Uses multi-model adversarial reasoning (10-15% accuracy gains, 30%+ factual error reduction) as a generic framework tool invocable by any SuperClaude command.\n    23→\n    24→## Required Input\n    25→\n    26→**MANDATORY**: Either `--compare` (Mode A), `--source`+`--generate`+`--agents` (Mode B), or `--pipeline` (Pipeline Mode) must be provided.\n    27→\n    28→```\n    29→/sc:adversarial --compare file1.md,file2.md[,...,fileN.md] [options]\n    30→/sc:adversarial --source <file> --generate <type> --agents <spec>[,...] [options]\n    31→/sc:adversarial --pipeline \"<shorthand>\" [options]\n    32→/sc:adversarial --pipeline @pipeline.yaml [options]\n    33→```\n    34→\n    35→## Triggers\n    36→\n    37→- Explicit: `/sc:adversarial --compare ...` or `/sc:adversarial --source ...`\n    38→- Keywords: \"adversarial debate\", \"compare variants\", \"merge best of\"\n    39→- Integration: Called by `/sc:roadmap` in multi-spec/multi-roadmap modes\n    40→\n    41→## Dual Input Modes\n    42→\n    43→### Mode A: Compare Existing Files (FR-001)\n    44→\n    45→```bash\n    46→/sc:adversarial --compare file1.md,file2.md[,...,file10.md]\n    47→```\n    48→\n    49→- Accepts 2-10 existing files for comparison, debate, and merge\n    50→- Files are copied to `<output>/adversarial/variant-N-original.md`\n    51→- Validation: All files must exist and be readable; 2-10 file count enforced\n    52→\n    53→### Mode B: Generate + Compare from Source (FR-001)\n    54→\n    55→```bash\n    56→/sc:adversarial --source source.md --generate <type> --agents <agent-spec>[,...]\n    57→```\n    58→\n    59→- Generates variant artifacts from source using specified agents in parallel\n    60→- Agent specification format: `<model>[:persona[:\"instruction\"]]`\n    61→  - `opus` — model only\n    62→  - `opus:architect` — model + persona\n    63→  - `opus:architect:\"focus on scalability\"` — model + persona + instruction\n    64→- Supported models: Any available (opus, sonnet, haiku, configured aliases)\n    65→- Agent count: 2-10 (minimum 2 required for adversarial comparison)\n    66→- Variants written to `<output>/adversarial/variant-N-<model>-<persona>.md`\n    67→\n    68→## 5-Step Adversarial Protocol (FR-002)\n    69→\n    70→The core pipeline executes 5 sequential steps. Each step produces a documented artifact.\n    71→\n    72→### Step 1: Diff Analysis\n    73→\n    74→**Input**: All variant artifacts\n    75→**Delegation**: Analytical agent (or /sc:analyze equivalent)\n    76→**Process**:\n    77→\n    78→```yaml\n    79→diff_analysis:\n    80→  structural_diff:\n    81→    action: \"Compare section ordering, hierarchy depth, heading structure across variants\"\n    82→    output: \"Structural differences with severity ratings (Low/Medium/High)\"\n    83→\n    84→  content_diff:\n    85→    action: \"Compare approaches topic-by-topic, identify coverage differences\"\n    86→    output: \"Content differences with approach summaries per variant\"\n    87→\n    88→  contradiction_detection:\n    89→    action: \"Structured scan per contradiction detection protocol\"\n    90→    categories:\n    91→      - \"Opposing claims about the same subject\"\n    92→      - \"Requirement-constraint conflicts\"\n    93→      - \"Impossible timeline/dependency sequences\"\n    94→    rule: \"Claims must be specific enough to be falsifiable\"\n    95→\n    96→  unique_contribution_extraction:\n    97→    action: \"Identify ideas present in only one variant\"\n    98→    output: \"Unique contributions with value assessment (Low/Medium/High)\"\n    99→\n   100→  shared_assumption_extraction:\n   101→    action: \"Identify agreement points across variants and extract implicit shared assumptions (AD-2)\"\n   102→    process:\n   103→      - \"Scan all diff categories for agreement points (where all variants converge)\"\n   104→      - \"For each agreement point, enumerate underlying preconditions\"\n   105→      - \"Classify each precondition as STATED/UNSTATED/CONTRADICTED\"\n   106→    output: \"Shared assumptions with classification and A-NNN identifiers\"\n   107→    promotion: \"UNSTATED preconditions become synthetic [SHARED-ASSUMPTION] diff points\"\n   108→```\n   109→\n   110→**Output**: `adversarial/diff-analysis.md` — organized by category with severity ratings, plus Shared Assumptions section\n   111→**Template**: See `refs/artifact-templates.md` Section 1\n   112→\n   113→### Step 2: Adversarial Debate\n   114→\n   115→**Input**: All variants + diff-analysis.md\n   116→**Delegation**: debate-orchestrator agent coordinates; advocate agents participate\n   117→**Process**:\n   118→\n   119→```yaml\n   120→adversarial_debate:\n   121→  debate_topic_taxonomy:\n   122→    purpose: \"Three-level taxonomy (AD-5) ensuring state-mechanics-level debate cannot be bypassed\"\n   123→    levels:\n   124→      L1_surface:\n   125→        description: \"Surface-level differences: naming, formatting, style, wording choices\"\n   126→        auto_tag_signals:\n   127→          - \"naming convention\"\n   128→          - \"formatting\"\n   129→          - \"wording\"\n   130→          - \"style choice\"\n   131→          - \"cosmetic\"\n   132→          - \"presentation\"\n   133→        examples: \"Variable naming, markdown formatting, section ordering preferences\"\n   134→\n   135→      L2_structural:\n   136→        description: \"Structural differences: architecture, organization, API design, component boundaries\"\n   137→        auto_tag_signals:\n   138→          - \"architecture\"\n   139→          - \"API design\"\n   140→          - \"component\"\n   141→          - \"module\"\n   142→          - \"interface\"\n   143→          - \"dependency\"\n   144→          - \"organization\"\n   145→        examples: \"Module decomposition, API endpoint design, data flow patterns\"\n   146→\n   147→      L3_state_mechanics:\n   148→        description: \"State-mechanics-level: state management, guard conditions, boundary validation, concurrency\"\n   149→        auto_tag_signals:\n   150→          - \"state\"\n   151→          - \"guard\"\n   152→          - \"boundary\"\n   153→          - \"invariant\"\n   154→          - \"concurrency\"\n   155→          - \"race condition\"\n   156→          - \"validation rule\"\n   157→          - \"transition\"\n   158→        examples: \"State machine transitions, input validation guards, concurrency controls\"\n   159→\n   160→    auto_tagging:\n   161→      action: \"Scan each diff point text for level-specific signal terms\"\n   162→      priority: \"L3 > L2 > L1 (if signals match multiple levels, assign highest)\"\n   163→      shared_assumption_rule: \"A-NNN points containing state/guard/boundary terms auto-tag as L3 (AC-AD5-3)\"\n   164→      fallback: \"If no signals match, assign L2 (structural) as default\"\n   165→      mutual_exclusivity: \"Each diff point receives exactly one taxonomy level\"\n   166→\n   167→  advocate_instantiation:\n   168→    action: \"Parse model[:persona[:\\\"instruction\\\"]] spec for each variant\"\n   169→    creation: \"Task agents with advocate prompts including variant + all others + diff-analysis\"\n   170→\n   171→  round_1_parallel:\n   172→    action: \"Each advocate presents strengths and critiques others\"\n   173→    execution: \"Parallel Task calls — all advocates run simultaneously\"\n   174→    steelman_requirement: \"Advocates MUST construct strongest version of opposing positions before critiquing\"\n   175→\n   176→  round_2_sequential:\n   177→    condition: \"--depth standard OR --depth deep\"\n   178→    action: \"Rebuttals — each advocate addresses criticisms from Round 1\"\n   179→    execution: \"Sequential — each receives all Round 1 transcripts\"\n   180→\n   181→  round_3_conditional:\n   182→    condition: \"--depth deep AND convergence < threshold\"\n   183→    action: \"Final arguments after considering all rebuttals\"\n   184→\n   185→  convergence_detection:\n   186→    metric: \"Percentage of diff points where agents agree on superior approach\"\n   187→    threshold: \"Configurable via --convergence (default 0.80)\"\n   188→    tracking: \"Per-point agreement tracking across rounds\"\n   189→\n   190→  scoring_matrix:\n   191→    action: \"For each diff point, record winner, confidence, evidence summary\"\n   192→    output: \"Per-point scoring matrix table\"\n   193→```\n   194→\n   195→**Output**: `adversarial/debate-transcript.md` — full debate with per-point scoring\n   196→**Template**: See `refs/artifact-templates.md` Section 2\n   197→\n   198→### Step 3: Hybrid Scoring & Base Selection\n   199→\n   200→**Input**: All variants + debate-transcript.md\n   201→**Delegation**: debate-orchestrator agent\n   202→**Process**:\n   203→\n   204→```yaml\n   205→base_selection:\n   206→  quantitative_layer:\n   207→    weight: 0.50\n   208→    metrics:\n   209→      requirement_coverage:\n   210→        weight: 0.30\n   211→        computation: \"grep-match source requirements in variant / total requirements\"\n   212→      internal_consistency:\n   213→        weight: 0.25\n   214→        computation: \"1 - (contradictions / total claims)\"\n   215→      specificity_ratio:\n   216→        weight: 0.15\n   217→        computation: \"concrete statements / total substantive statements\"\n   218→      dependency_completeness:\n   219→        weight: 0.15\n   220→        computation: \"resolved internal references / total internal references\"\n   221→      section_coverage:\n   222→        weight: 0.15\n   223→        computation: \"variant sections / max(sections across all variants)\"\n   224→    formula: \"quant_score = (RC×0.30) + (IC×0.25) + (SR×0.15) + (DC×0.15) + (SC×0.15)\"\n   225→\n   226→  qualitative_layer:\n   227→    weight: 0.50\n   228→    rubric: \"25-criterion additive binary rubric across 5 dimensions\"\n   229→    dimensions:\n   230→      - \"Completeness (5 criteria)\"\n   231→      - \"Correctness (5 criteria)\"\n   232→      - \"Structure (5 criteria)\"\n   233→      - \"Clarity (5 criteria)\"\n   234→      - \"Risk Coverage (5 criteria)\"\n   235→    evidence_protocol: \"Claim-Evidence-Verdict (CEV) for every criterion\"\n   236→    formula: \"qual_score = total_criteria_met / 25\"\n   237→\n   238→  position_bias_mitigation:\n   239→    pass_1: \"Evaluate variants in input order (A, B, C, ...)\"\n   240→    pass_2: \"Evaluate variants in reverse order (C, B, A, ...)\"\n   241→    agreement: \"Use agreed verdict\"\n   242→    disagreement: \"Re-evaluate with explicit comparison prompt citing both passes\"\n   243→\n   244→  combined_scoring:\n   245→    formula: \"variant_score = (0.50 × quant_score) + (0.50 × qual_score)\"\n   246→\n   247→  tiebreaker_protocol:\n   248→    condition: \"Top two variants within 5% of each other\"\n   249→    level_1: \"Debate performance (points won in Step 2)\"\n   250→    level_2: \"Higher correctness criteria count\"\n   251→    level_3: \"Input order (arbitrary but deterministic)\"\n   252→```\n   253→\n   254→**Output**: `adversarial/base-selection.md` — full scoring breakdown with evidence\n   255→**Template**: See `refs/artifact-templates.md` Section 3\n   256→**Reference**: See `refs/scoring-protocol.md` for complete algorithm\n   257→\n   258→### Step 4: Refactoring Plan\n   259→\n   260→**Input**: Selected base + all other variants + debate-transcript.md\n   261→**Delegation**: debate-orchestrator drafts, analytical agent reviews\n   262→**Process**:\n   263→\n   264→```yaml\n   265→refactoring_plan:\n   266→  for_each_non_base_strength:\n   267→    action: \"Generate improvement description + integration point\"\n   268→    fields:\n   269→      - \"Source variant and section\"\n   270→      - \"Target location in base\"\n   271→      - \"Rationale (citing debate evidence)\"\n   272→      - \"Integration approach\"\n   273→      - \"Risk level\"\n   274→\n   275→  for_each_base_weakness:\n   276→    action: \"Reference which non-base variant addresses it better\"\n   277→    fields:\n   278→      - \"Issue description\"\n   279→      - \"Better variant reference\"\n   280→      - \"Fix approach\"\n   281→\n   282→  changes_not_being_made:\n   283→    action: \"Document differences where base approach was determined superior\"\n   284→    rationale: \"Transparency — show what was considered and rejected\"\n   285→\n   286→  review:\n   287→    default: \"Auto-approved\"\n   288→    interactive: \"User-approved when --interactive flag set\"\n   289→```\n   290→\n   291→**Output**: `adversarial/refactor-plan.md` — actionable merge plan with integration points\n   292→**Template**: See `refs/artifact-templates.md` Section 4\n   293→\n   294→### Step 5: Merge Execution\n   295→\n   296→**Input**: Base variant + refactor-plan.md\n   297→**Delegation**: merge-executor agent (dedicated specialist)\n   298→**Process**:\n   299→\n   300→```yaml\n   301→merge_execution:\n   302→  step_1: \"Read base variant and refactoring plan\"\n   303→  step_2: \"Apply each planned change methodically\"\n   304→  step_3: \"Maintain structural integrity during merge\"\n   305→  step_4: \"Add provenance annotations (source attribution per section)\"\n   306→  step_5: \"Validate merged output — structural integrity, internal references, contradiction re-scan\"\n   307→  step_6: \"Produce merge-log.md documenting each applied change\"\n   308→```\n   309→\n   310→**Output**: Unified merged artifact + `adversarial/merge-log.md`\n   311→**Template**: See `refs/artifact-templates.md` Section 5\n   312→\n   313→## Configurable Parameters (FR-003)\n   314→\n   315→| Parameter | Flag | Default | Range | Description |\n   316→|-----------|------|---------|-------|-------------|\n   317→| Depth | `--depth` | `standard` | quick/standard/deep | Controls debate rounds (1/2/3) |\n   318→| Convergence | `--convergence` | `0.80` | 0.50-0.99 | Alignment threshold for deep mode |\n   319→| Interactive | `--interactive` | `false` | true/false | User approval at decision points |\n   320→| Output dir | `--output` | Auto-derived | Any path | Where artifacts are written |\n   321→| Focus areas | `--focus` | All | Comma-separated | Debate focus areas |\n   322→| Pipeline | `--pipeline` | (none) | Inline shorthand or @path.yaml | Routes to Meta-Orchestrator; mutually exclusive with --compare/--source |\n   323→\n   324→## Interactive Mode (FR-004)\n   325→\n   326→When `--interactive` is specified, the pipeline pauses for user input at:\n   327→\n   328→```yaml\n   329→interactive_checkpoints:\n   330→  after_diff_analysis:\n   331→    pause: \"User can highlight priority areas for debate\"\n   332→    default_action: \"Auto-proceed with all diff points\"\n   333→\n   334→  after_debate:\n   335→    pause: \"User can override convergence assessment\"\n   336→    default_action: \"Accept computed convergence\"\n   337→\n   338→  after_base_selection:\n   339→    pause: \"User can override the selected base\"\n   340→    default_action: \"Accept highest-scoring variant\"\n   341→\n   342→  after_refactoring_plan:\n   343→    pause: \"User can modify the plan before execution\"\n   344→    default_action: \"Auto-approve and execute\"\n   345→```\n   346→\n   347→Default (non-interactive): All decisions auto-resolved with rationale documented.\n   348→\n   349→## Artifact Output Structure (FR-005)\n   350→\n   351→```\n   352→<output-dir>/\n   353→├── <merged-output>.md              # Final unified artifact\n   354→└── adversarial/\n   355→    ├── variant-1-<agent>.md        # Variant 1\n   356→    ├── variant-2-<agent>.md        # Variant 2\n   357→    ├── ...                         # Up to 10 variants\n   358→    ├── diff-analysis.md            # Step 1 output\n   359→    ├── debate-transcript.md        # Step 2 output\n   360→    ├── base-selection.md           # Step 3 output\n   361→    ├── refactor-plan.md            # Step 4 output\n   362→    └── merge-log.md                # Step 5 execution log\n   363→```\n   364→\n   365→**Naming conventions**:\n   366→- Mode A: `variant-N-original.md` (copies of input files)\n   367→- Mode B: `variant-N-<model>-<persona>.md`\n   368→\n   369→## Error Handling Matrix (FR-006)\n   370→\n   371→```yaml\n   372→error_handling:\n   373→  agent_failure:\n   374→    behavior: \"Retry once, then proceed with N-1 variants\"\n   375→    constraint: \"Minimum 2 variants required\"\n   376→    fallback: \"If only 1 variant survives, abort and return it with warning\"\n   377→\n   378→  variants_too_similar:\n   379→    threshold: \"<10% diff\"\n   380→    behavior: \"Skip debate, select either as base\"\n   381→    log: \"variants substantially identical\"\n   382→\n   383→  no_convergence:\n   384→    condition: \"Max rounds reached without meeting threshold\"\n   385→    behavior: \"Force-select by score, document non-convergence\"\n   386→    flag: \"Flag for user review\"\n   387→\n   388→  merge_failure:\n   389→    behavior: \"Preserve all artifacts, flag failure\"\n   390→    recovery: \"Provide refactor-plan.md for manual execution\"\n   391→\n   392→  single_variant_remaining:\n   393→    behavior: \"Abort adversarial process\"\n   394→    return: \"Surviving variant as-is with warning\"\n   395→```\n   396→\n   397→## Return Contract (MANDATORY)\n   398→\n   399→**This is the final pipeline step.** sc:adversarial MUST write this return contract on every invocation, including failures. When a field's value cannot be determined (pipeline aborted before reaching that step), use `null`.\n   400→\n   401→When invoked by another command, sc:adversarial returns:\n   402→\n   403→```yaml\n   404→return_contract:\n   405→  merged_output_path: \"<path to merged file>\"       # null if merge not reached\n   406→  convergence_score: 0.75                            # float 0.0-1.0, null if debate not reached\n   407→  artifacts_dir: \"<path to adversarial/ directory>\"  # always set (created at pipeline start)\n   408→  status: \"success\"                                  # success | partial | failed\n   409→  base_variant: \"opus:architect\"                     # model:persona that won debate, null if not reached\n   410→  unresolved_conflicts: 2                            # integer count of unresolved diff points, 0 on success\n   411→  fallback_mode: false                               # true if pipeline used fallback path\n   412→  failure_stage: null                                # null on success; \"variant_generation\" | \"debate\" | \"merge\" | \"validation\" | \"transport\"\n   413→  invocation_method: \"skill-direct\"                  # \"skill-direct\" | \"task-agent\" | \"manual\"\n   414→```\n   415→\n   416→**Write-on-failure**: If the pipeline aborts at any stage, sc:adversarial MUST still write the return contract with `status: \"failed\"`, the `failure_stage` set to the step that failed, and all unreached fields set to `null`. This ensures the caller can always consume the contract.\n   417→\n   418→**Field definitions**:\n   419→\n   420→| Field | Type | Description |\n   421→|-------|------|-------------|\n   422→| `merged_output_path` | `string\\|null` | Path to merged output file; null if merge not reached |\n   423→| `convergence_score` | `float\\|null` | Final convergence score 0.0-1.0; null if debate not reached |\n   424→| `artifacts_dir` | `string` | Path to adversarial/ directory (always set) |\n   425→| `status` | `enum` | `success` (all steps pass), `partial` (completed with warnings), `failed` (aborted) |\n   426→| `base_variant` | `string\\|null` | Model:persona that won adversarial debate; null if debate not reached |\n   427→| `unresolved_conflicts` | `integer` | Count of unresolved diff points; 0 on full success |\n   428→| `fallback_mode` | `boolean` | True if pipeline used any fallback path |\n   429→| `failure_stage` | `string\\|null` | Null on success; identifies the stage that caused failure |\n   430→| `invocation_method` | `enum` | How this invocation was triggered: `skill-direct`, `task-agent`, or `manual` |\n   431→\n   432→## Agent Delegation\n   433→\n   434→### debate-orchestrator Agent\n   435→- **Role**: Coordinates the entire pipeline without participating in debates\n   436→- **Model**: Highest-capability (opus preferred)\n   437→- **Tools**: Task, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Bash\n   438→- **Responsibilities**: Parse inputs, dispatch variants, coordinate 5-step protocol, track convergence, make base selection, compile return contract\n   439→- **Does NOT**: Generate variants, participate in debates, execute merges\n   440→\n   441→### merge-executor Agent\n   442→- **Role**: Executes refactoring plans to produce unified merged artifacts\n   443→- **Model**: High-capability (opus or sonnet)\n   444→- **Tools**: Read, Write, Edit, Grep\n   445→- **Responsibilities**: Read base + plan, apply changes, maintain integrity, add provenance, validate output, produce merge-log\n   446→- **Does NOT**: Make strategic decisions, override plan, participate in debates\n   447→\n   448→### Advocate Agents (Dynamic)\n   449→- **Role**: Argue for their variant's strengths in structured debate\n   450→- **Instantiation**: Dynamic from `--agents` specification\n   451→- **Behavior**: Shaped by model + persona + instruction; steelman opposing positions before critiquing\n   452→\n   453→## MCP Integration\n   454→\n   455→| Server | Usage | Steps |\n   456→|--------|-------|-------|\n   457→| Sequential | Debate scoring, convergence analysis, refactoring plan logic | Steps 2-4 |\n   458→| Serena | Memory persistence of adversarial outcomes | Step 5 |\n   459→| Context7 | Domain pattern validation during merge | Step 5 |\n   460→\n   461→**Circuit breaker**: If Sequential unavailable, fall back to native Claude reasoning with depth reduction (deep → standard, standard → quick).\n   462→\n   463→## Compliance Tier Classification\n   464→\n   465→Default tier: **STRICT** — adversarial debate involves multi-file operations, multi-agent coordination, and complex scoring.\n   466→\n   467→Automatic escalation triggers:\n   468→- Always STRICT when operating (multi-file by nature)\n   469→- Multi-agent delegation inherently complex\n   470→\n   471→## Boundaries\n   472→\n   473→### Will Do\n   474→- Compare 2-10 artifacts through structured adversarial debate\n   475→- Generate variant artifacts using different model/persona configurations\n   476→- Produce transparent, documented merge decisions\n   477→- Execute refactoring plans to produce unified outputs\n   478→- Support configurable depth, convergence thresholds, and focus areas\n   479→- Work as a generic tool invocable by any SuperClaude command\n   480→\n   481→### Will Not Do\n   482→- Validate domain-specific correctness of merged output (calling command's responsibility)\n   483→- Execute the merged output (planning tool, not execution tool)\n   484→- Manage git operations or version control\n   485→- Make decisions without documented rationale\n   486→- Operate with fewer than 2 variants (minimum for adversarial comparison)\n   487→- Override user decisions in interactive mode\n   488→\n   489→---\n   490→\n   491→## Implementation Details — Step 1: Diff Analysis Engine\n   492→\n   493→### Input Mode Parsing Protocol\n 575→\n   576→### Variant File Loading and Normalization\n   577→\n   578→After input mode parsing, load and normalize variants:\n   579→\n   580→```yaml\n   581→variant_loading:\n   582→  output_directory:\n   583→    creation: \"Create <output-dir>/adversarial/ if it does not exist\"\n   584→    structure: |\n   585→      <output-dir>/\n   586→      └── adversarial/\n   587→          ├── variant-1-<suffix>.md\n   588→          ├── variant-2-<suffix>.md\n   589→          └── ...\n   590→\n   591→  mode_a_loading:\n   592→    action: \"Read each input file and copy to adversarial directory\"\n   593→    naming: \"variant-N-original.md (N = 1-based index in input order)\"\n   594→    process:\n   595→      - \"For file_index, file_path in enumerate(input_files, start=1):\"\n   596→      - \"  Read file content\"\n   597→      - \"  Write to <output-dir>/adversarial/variant-{file_index}-original.md\"\n   598→    normalization:\n   599→      - \"Strip trailing whitespace from each line\"\n   600→      - \"Ensure file ends with single newline\"\n   601→      - \"Preserve original heading structure exactly\"\n   602→\n   603→  mode_b_loading:\n   604→    action: \"Placeholder — variant generation handled in Phase 6 (T06.02)\"\n   605→    naming: \"variant-N-<model>-<persona>.md\"\n   606→    stub_behavior: |\n   607→      Mode B variant generation is wired in Phase 6.\n   608→      When reached, dispatch Task agents per --agents spec.\n   609→      Each agent generates an artifact from --source using --generate type.\n   610→      Results written to adversarial/ with naming convention above.\n   611→\n   612→  variant_metadata:\n   613→    track_per_variant:\n   614→      - \"variant_id: N (1-based)\"\n   615→      - \"source_path: original file path (Mode A) or 'generated' (Mode B)\"\n   616→      - \"agent_spec: agent specification (Mode B only)\"\n   617→      - \"heading_count: number of top-level sections\"\n   618→      - \"line_count: total lines\"\n   619→      - \"word_count: approximate word count\"\n   620→```\n   621→\n   622→### Structural Diff Engine\n   623→\n   624→Compare heading hierarchies, section ordering, and structural organization:\n   625→\n   626→```yaml\n   627→structural_diff:\n   628→  heading_extraction:\n   629→    process:\n   630→      - \"Scan each variant for markdown heading lines (^#{1,6} )\"\n   631→      - \"Build heading tree: level, text, line number, children\"\n   632→      - \"Record: max_depth, heading_count_per_level, section_sequence\"\n   633→    heading_levels:\n   634→      H1: \"# (document title)\"\n   635→      H2: \"## (top-level sections)\"\n   636→      H3: \"### (subsections)\"\n   637→      H4_plus: \"#### and deeper (detail sections)\"\n   638→\n   639→  comparison_dimensions:\n   640→    section_ordering:\n   641→      action: \"Compare sequence of H2 headings across variants\"\n   642→      match: \"Fuzzy match section names (≥80% word overlap = same topic)\"\n   643→      output: \"Ordered list of sections per variant, highlighting order differences\"\n   644→      severity:\n   645→        Low: \"Same sections, different order (cosmetic preference)\"\n   646→        Medium: \"Different grouping or categorization of topics\"\n   647→        High: \"Incompatible organizational models (e.g., chronological vs. categorical)\"\n   648→\n   649→    hierarchy_depth:\n   650→      action: \"Compare max nesting level per variant\"\n   651→      output: \"Max depth per variant, per-section depth comparison\"\n   652→      severity:\n   653→        Low: \"Depth differs by 1 level\"\n   654→        Medium: \"Depth differs by 2+ levels\"\n   655→        High: \"One variant uses flat structure, other uses deep nesting\"\n   656→\n   657→    heading_structure:\n   658→      action: \"Compare heading count and distribution across levels\"\n   659→      output: \"Heading count per level per variant\"\n   660→      severity:\n   661→        Low: \"Minor differences in subsection count\"\n   662→        Medium: \"Significant differences in section granularity\"\n   663→        High: \"Fundamentally different document organization\"\n   664→\n   665→  output_format:\n   666→    id_scheme: \"S-NNN (sequential, starting at S-001)\"\n   667→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Area\", \"Variant A\", \"Variant B\", \"...\", \"Severity\"]\n   668→    scaling: \"For >2 variants, expand table horizontally with one column per variant\"\n   669→```\n   670→\n   671→### Content Diff Engine\n   672→\n   673→Compare approaches topic-by-topic across variants:\n   674→\n   675→```yaml\n   676→content_diff:\n   677→  topic_extraction:\n   678→    process:\n   679→      - \"For each variant, extract topics from H2/H3 section headings\"\n   680→      - \"Build topic inventory: {topic_name, variant_id, section_ref, content_summary}\"\n   681→      - \"Match topics across variants using fuzzy name matching (≥60% word overlap)\"\n   682→    categorize:\n   683→      shared_topics: \"Topics present in 2+ variants\"\n   684→      variant_only_topics: \"Topics in exactly one variant (→ feeds unique contributions)\"\n   685→\n   686→  approach_comparison:\n   687→    for_shared_topics:\n   688→      action: \"For each matched topic, compare how variants address it\"\n   689→      dimensions:\n   690→        - \"Coverage depth: how thoroughly the topic is addressed\"\n   691→        - \"Approach: what strategy or method is proposed\"\n   692→        - \"Detail level: specificity of recommendations or requirements\"\n   693→        - \"Emphasis: what aspects are prioritized\"\n   694→      output: \"Per-topic comparison with approach summaries per variant\"\n   695→\n   696→    coverage_gaps:\n   697→      action: \"Identify topics covered by some variants but not others\"\n   698→      output: \"Gap matrix showing which topics are missing from which variants\"\n   699→\n   700→  severity_assignment:\n   701→    Low: \"Same conclusion via different wording or emphasis\"\n   702→    Medium: \"Materially different approaches to the same topic\"\n   703→    High: \"Fundamentally incompatible strategies for the same topic\"\n   704→\n   705→  output_format:\n   706→    id_scheme: \"C-NNN (sequential, starting at C-001)\"\n   707→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Topic\", \"Variant A Approach\", \"Variant B Approach\", \"...\", \"Severity\"]\n   708→```\n   709→\n   710→### Contradiction Detection Protocol\n   711→\n   712→Structured scan for contradictions across and within variants:\n   713→\n   714→```yaml\n   715→contradiction_detection:\n   716→  claim_extraction:\n   717→    process:\n   718→      - \"Scan each variant for specific, falsifiable statements\"\n   719→      - \"A claim is falsifiable if it can be definitively proven true or false\"\n   720→      - \"EXCLUDE vague statements: 'as appropriate', 'as needed', 'best practices' (without citation)\"\n   721→      - \"EXCLUDE subjective preferences without criteria\"\n   722→    claim_types:\n   723→      factual: \"Assertions about facts, numbers, dates, technologies\"\n   724→      requirement: \"Statements of what must/shall/should be done\"\n   725→      constraint: \"Limitations, thresholds, boundaries\"\n   726→      dependency: \"Sequential requirements, prerequisites, timelines\"\n   727→\n   728→  contradiction_categories:\n   729→    opposing_claims:\n   730→      description: \"Two statements assert opposite or incompatible things about the same subject\"\n   731→      detection: \"For each claim, search other claims for negation or incompatible assertion about the same entity\"\n   732→      example: \"Variant A: 'Use PostgreSQL' vs Variant B: 'Use MongoDB for the same data store'\"\n   733→      scope: \"Both cross-variant and intra-variant (within same document)\"\n   734→\n   735→    requirement_constraint_conflicts:\n   736→      description: \"A stated requirement conflicts with a stated constraint\"\n   737→      detection: \"Match requirement statements against constraint statements; flag where satisfying one violates the other\"\n   738→      example: \"'Must support 10K concurrent users' vs 'Single-threaded architecture required'\"\n   739→\n   740→    impossible_sequences:\n   741→      description: \"Timeline or dependency creates an impossible execution order\"\n   742→      detection: \"Build dependency graph from stated prerequisites; detect cycles or impossible orderings\"\n   743→      example: \"'Module A depends on Module B' AND 'Module B depends on Module A'\"\n   744→\n   745→  severity_assignment:\n   746→    Low: \"Minor wording conflict, does not affect implementation\"\n   747→    Medium: \"Substantive disagreement that affects design decisions\"\n   748→    High: \"Fundamental incompatibility that must be resolved before merge\"\n   749→\n   750→  output_format:\n   751→    id_scheme: \"X-NNN (sequential, starting at X-001)\"\n   752→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Point of Conflict\", \"Variant A Position\", \"Variant B Position\", \"...\", \"Impact\"]\n   753→    evidence_requirement: \"Each contradiction must cite specific text from both variants\"\n   754→```\n   755→\n   756→### Unique Contribution Extraction\n   757→\n   758→Identify ideas present in only one variant:\n   759→\n   760→```yaml\n   761→unique_contribution_extraction:\n   762→  detection:\n   763→    process:\n   764→      - \"For each section/idea in each variant, check if ANY other variant covers the same topic\"\n   765→      - \"Use topic matching from content diff engine (≥60% word overlap = covered)\"\n   766→      - \"Ideas with NO match in any other variant = unique contribution\"\n   767→    granularity: \"Section-level (H2 or H3) — not individual sentences\"\n   768→    exclusions:\n   769→      - \"Boilerplate sections common to the artifact type (table of contents, metadata)\"\n   770→      - \"Minor formatting or stylistic choices\"\n   771→      - \"Restatements of input requirements (not original contributions)\"\n   772→\n   773→  value_assessment:\n   774→    High: \"Addresses a gap no other variant covers; high impact on completeness or correctness\"\n   775→    Medium: \"Useful addition that improves quality but is not critical\"\n   776→    Low: \"Nice to have; minimal impact on overall artifact quality\"\n   777→\n   778→  assessment_criteria:\n   779→    - \"Does this address a requirement not covered elsewhere?\"\n   780→    - \"Does this add risk mitigation not present in other variants?\"\n   781→    - \"Does this improve clarity or usability significantly?\"\n   782→    - \"Is this a novel approach that could strengthen the merged output?\"\n   783→\n   784→  output_format:\n   785→    id_scheme: \"U-NNN (sequential, starting at U-001)\"\n   786→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Variant\", \"Contribution\", \"Value Assessment\"]\n   787→```\n   788→\n   789→### Shared Assumption Extraction Engine (AD-2)\n   790→\n   791→Addresses the \"agreement = no scrutiny\" blind spot by extracting implicit shared assumptions from variant agreement points:\n   792→\n   793→```yaml\n   794→shared_assumption_extraction:\n   795→  purpose: \"Surface hidden preconditions that all variants assume without stating (AD-2)\"\n   796→  integration_point: \"Runs after unique_contribution_extraction, before diff-analysis.md assembly\"\n   797→\n   798→  agreement_identification:\n   799→    action: \"Scan all diff categories (S-NNN, C-NNN, X-NNN, U-NNN) for convergence\"\n   800→    convergence_definition: |\n   801→      An agreement point exists when:\n   802→      - All variants take the same approach to a topic (content diff)\n   803→      - All variants use the same structure for a concept (structural diff)\n   804→      - No contradiction exists between variants on a subject (absence from X-NNN)\n   805→    output: \"List of agreement points with category and description\"\n   806→\n   807→  assumption_enumeration:\n   808→    action: \"For each agreement point, enumerate underlying preconditions\"\n   809→    technique: |\n   810→      Ask: 'What must be true for this agreement to be valid?'\n   811→      Categories of assumptions:\n   812→      - Domain assumptions: 'The system uses X architecture'\n   813→      - Scale assumptions: 'Traffic will not exceed Y'\n   814→      - Dependency assumptions: 'Library Z will be available'\n   815→      - Business assumptions: 'Requirement R is correctly understood'\n   816→      - Technical assumptions: 'Pattern P is applicable here'\n   817→    output: \"List of preconditions per agreement point\"\n   818→\n   819→  classification:\n   820→    STATED: \"Precondition is explicitly mentioned in at least one variant\"\n   821→    UNSTATED: \"Precondition is implicit — no variant states it, but all depend on it\"\n   822→    CONTRADICTED: \"Precondition is inconsistent — variants depend on it but evidence contradicts it\"\n   823→\n   824→  promotion_to_diff_points:\n   825→    condition: \"classification == UNSTATED\"\n   826→    action: \"Promote to synthetic [SHARED-ASSUMPTION] diff point\"\n   827→    id_scheme: \"A-NNN (sequential, starting at A-001 per debate session)\"\n   828→    tag: \"[SHARED-ASSUMPTION]\"\n   829→    inclusion: \"Added to 1093→\n  1094→```yaml\n  1095→convergence_detection:\n  1096→  metric: \"Percentage of diff points where advocates agree on superior approach\"\n  1097→  formula: \"convergence = agreed_points / total_diff_points\"\n  1098→  total_diff_points_calculation: |\n  1099→    total_diff_points = count(S-NNN) + count(C-NNN) + count(X-NNN) + count(A-NNN)\n  1100→    Note: A-NNN (shared assumption) points are included in the denominator (AD-2, D-0012).\n  1101→    Debates without shared assumptions produce identical convergence scores (backward compatible).\n  1102→  threshold:\n  1103→    default: 0.80\n  1104→    configurable: \"--convergence flag (range 0.50-0.99)\"\n  1105→    validation: \"If value outside range, warn and use default 0.80\"\n  1106→\n  1107→  per_point_tracking:\n  1108→    data_structure:\n  1109→      point_id: \"Diff point ID (S-NNN, C-NNN, X-NNN, A-NNN)\"\n  1110→      taxonomy_level: \"L1 | L2 | L3 (from debate_topic_taxonomy auto-tagging)\"\n  1111→      round_1_positions: \"{variant_id: position}\"\n  1112→      round_2_positions: \"{variant_id: position}\"\n  1113→      round_3_positions: \"{variant_id: position}\"\n  1114→      agreed: \"true/false\"\n  1115→      winner: \"variant_id or null\"\n  1116→\n  1117→  taxonomy_coverage_gate:\n  1118→    purpose: \"AD-5: Ensure state-mechanics-level debate cannot be bypassed\"\n  1119→    check_timing: \"After each debate round, before convergence assessment\"\n  1120→    algorithm: |\n  1121→      1. Count diff points per taxonomy level: L1_count, L2_count, L3_count\n  1122→      2. Check coverage: all three levels must have > 0 points addressed\n  1123→      3. If any level has zero coverage:\n  1124→         a. Block convergence regardless of score\n  1125→         b. Trigger forced round for uncovered level\n  1126→    gate_condition: \"convergence requires: (all_levels_covered == true) AND (score >= threshold)\"\n  1127→    forced_round_trigger:\n  1128→      condition: \"Any taxonomy level has zero coverage after a debate round\"\n  1129→      action: |\n  1130→        1. Identify uncovered level(s)\n  1131→        2. Dispatch forced debate round targeting the uncovered level\n  1132→        3. Forced round uses level-specific prompt focusing on that level's concerns\n  1133→        4. Still triggers at depth=quick when coverage is zero (AC-AD5-4)\n  1134→      prompt_template: |\n  1135→        FORCED ROUND: Taxonomy Level {level} has zero coverage.\n  1136→        The debate has not addressed any {level_description} concerns.\n  1137→        Please identify and debate at least one {level} issue from the diff analysis.\n  1138→        Focus on: {level_auto_tag_signals}\n  1139→      log: \"Forced round triggered: {level} has zero coverage (AC-AD5-1)\"\n  1140→\n  1141→  agreement_determination:\n  1142→    unanimous: \"All advocates agree on same winner → agreed=true\"\n  1143→    majority: \"≥2/3 of advocates agree → agreed=true (winner = majority choice)\"\n  1144→    split: \"No majority → agreed=false (point remains unresolved)\"\n  1145→\n  1146→  early_termination_conditions:\n  1147→    unanimous_agreement:\n  1148→      condition: \"All points have unanimous agreement AND all taxonomy levels covered\"\n  1149→      action: \"Terminate debate immediately\"\n  1150→      log: \"Convergence: 100% (unanimous)\"\n  1151→\n  1152→    stable_majority:\n  1153→      condition: \"≥threshold agreement maintained for 2 consecutive rounds AND all taxonomy levels covered\"\n  1154→      action: \"Terminate debate\"\n  1155→      log: \"Convergence: {N}% (stable majority over 2 rounds)\"\n  1156→\n  1157→    max_rounds:\n  1158→      condition: \"Maximum rounds reached for configured depth\"\n  1159→      action: \"Terminate debate\"\n  1160→      log: \"Convergence: {N}% (max rounds reached)\"\n  1161→\n  1162→    oscillation_detection:\n  1163→      condition: \"Same points flip winner between rounds without resolving\"\n  1164→      action: \"Terminate debate with flag\"\n  1165→      log: \"Convergence: {N}% (oscillation detected on points: {list})\"\n  1166→\n  1167→  status_output:\n  1168→    CONVERGED: \"Agreement ≥ threshold AND all taxonomy levels covered\"\n  1169→    NOT_CONVERGED: \"Agreement < threshold after max rounds\"\n  1170→    BLOCKED_BY_TAXONOMY: \"Agreement ≥ threshold BUT taxonomy level {level} has zero coverage\"\n  1171→```\n  1172→\n  1173→### Per-Point Scoring Matrix (T03.06)\n  1174→\n  1175→```yaml\n  1176→scoring_matrix:\n  1177→  purpose: \"Record debate outcomes per diff point for base selection in Step 3\"\n  1178→\n  1179→  per_point_entry:\n  1180→    diff_point_id: \"From confidence\"\n  1189→    split: \"If no majority → mark as unresolved with 50% confidence\"\n  1190→    concession_boost: \"If losing advocate conceded the point → +10% confidence\"\n  1191→\n  1192→  confidence_calibration:\n  1193→    rules:\n  1194→      - \"Never assign 100% unless ALL advocates explicitly conceded\"\n  1195→      - \"Never assign <50% (that would indicate the other variant \"70-89%\": \"Clear majority with supporting evidence\"\n  1200→      \"50-69%\": \"Contested point with slight edge\"\n  1201→\n  1202→  output_format:\n  1203→    table: |\n  1204→      | Diff Point evidence |\n  1207→      | C-001 | Variant B | 72% | More thorough coverage; Variant A advocate conceded |\n  1208→```\n  1209→\n  1210→### debate-transcript.md Artifact Assembly (T03.07)\n  1211→\n Scoring Layer (T04.01)\n  1268→\n  1269→5 deterministic metrics computed from artifact text (no LLM judgment):\n  1270→\n  1271→```yaml\n  1272→quantitative_scoring:\n  1273→  metrics:\n  1274→    requirement_coverage:\n  1275→      symbol: \"RC\"\n  1276→      weight: 0.30\n  1277→      computation:\n  1278→        step_1: \"Extract requirement IDs from source input (FR-XXX, NFR-XXX, R-XXX match)\"\n  1281→        step_4: \"RC = matched_requirements / total_source_requirements\"\n  1282→      edge_case: \"If source has no formal requirement IDs, use section-level topic matching\"\n  1283→\n  1284→    internal_consistency:\n  1285→      symbol: \"IC\"\n  1286→      weight: 0.25\n  1287→      computation:\n  1288→        step_1: \"Extract all scorable claims (specific, falsifiable statements)\"\n  1289→        step_2: \"For each claim, search for contradicting claims within same variant\"\n  1290→        step_3: \"Contradiction categories: opposing claims, requirement-constraint conflicts, impossible sequences\"\n  1291→        step_4: \"IC = 1 - (contradiction_count / total_claims)\"\n  1292→      rule: \"Vague statements ('as appropriate', 'as needed') are NOT scorable claims\"\n  1293→      reuse: \"Leverages contradiction detection from Step 1 (T02.05)\"\n  1294→\n  1295→    specificity_ratio:\n  1296→      symbol: \"SR\"\n  1297→      weight: 0.15\n  1298→      computation:\n  1299→        concrete_indicators:\n  1300→          - \"Numbers and quantities ('5 milestones', '80% threshold')\"\n  1301→          - \"Dates and timeframes ('2-week sprint', 'by Q3')\"\n  1302→          - \"Named entities ('PostgreSQL', 'OAuth2', 'WCAG 2.1')\"\n  1303→          - \"Specific thresholds ('<200ms', '≥99.9%')\"\n  1304→          - \"Measurable criteria ('zero critical vulnerabilities')\"\n  1305→        vague_indicators:\n  1306→          - \"'appropriate', 'as needed', 'properly', 'adequate'\"\n  1307→          - \"'should consider', 'might', 'various', 'etc.'\"\n  1308→          - \"'best practices', 'industry standard' (without citation)\"\n  1309→        excluded: \"Headings, boilerplate, metadata lines\"\n  1310→        formula: \"SR = concrete_count / (concrete_count + vague_count)\"\n  1311→\n  1312→    dependency_completeness:\n  1313→      symbol: \"DC\"\n  1314→      weight: 0.15\n  1315→      computation:\n  1316→        step_1: \"Scan for internal references (section refs, milestone refs, component refs)\"\n  1317→        step_2: \"For each reference, check if the referenced item is defined elsewhere in the document\"\n  1318→        step_3: \"DC = resolved_references / total_references\"\n  1319→      reference_patterns:\n  1320→        - \"Section X.Y references\"\n  1321→        - \"Milestone M{N} references\"\n  1322→        - \"Deliverable D{M}.{N} references\"\n  1323→        - \"'See [section name]' cross-references\"\n  1324→      edge_case: \"External references (URLs, other documents) are EXCLUDED\"\n  1325→\n  1326→    section_coverage:\n  1327→      symbol: \"SC\"\n  1328→      weight: 0.15\n  1329→      computation:\n  1330→        step_1: \"Count top-level sections (H2 headings) in each variant\"\n  1331→        step_2: \"Find max section count across all variants\"\n  1332→        step_3: \"SC = variant_section_count / max_section_count\"\n  1333→      note: \"Normalized so at least one variant always scores 1.0\"\n  1334→\n  1335→  formula: \"quant_score = (RC × 0.30) + (IC × 0.25) + (SR × 0.15) + (DC × 0.15) + (SC × 0.15)\"\n  1336→  range: \"[0.0, 1.0]\"\n  1337→  determinism: \"Running twice on same input MUST produce identical scores\"\n  1338→```\n  1339→\n  1340→### Qualitative Scoring Layer (T04.02)\n  1341→\n  1342→25-criterion additive binary rubric with mandatory evidence citation:\n  1343→\n  1344→```yaml\n  1345→qualitative_scoring:\n  1346→  rubric:\n  1347→    completeness:\n  1348→      criteria:\n  1349→        1: \"Covers        4: \"No internal contradictions (cross-validated with IC metric)\"\n  1361→        5: \"Claims supported by evidence or rationale within the document\"\n  1362→\n  1363→    structure:\n  1364→      criteria:\n  1365→        1: \"Logical section ordering (prerequisites before dependents)\"\n  1366→        2: \"Consistent hierarchy depth (no orphaned subsections)\"\n  1367→        3: \"Clear abstract (specific actions, not general principles)\"\n  1375→        3: \"Each section has a clear purpose or can be summarized in one sentence\"\n  1376→        4: \"Acronyms and domain terms defined on first use\"\n  1377→        5: \"Actionable next steps or decision points clearly identified\"\n  1378→\n  1379→    risk_coverage:\n  1380→      criteria:\n  1381→        1: \"Identifies at procedures\"\n  1384→        4: \"Considers external dependencies and their failure scenarios\"\n  1385→        5: \"Includes monitoring or validation mechanism for risk detection\"\n  1386→\n  1387→  evidence_protocol:\n  1388→    name: \"Claim-Evidence-Verdict (CEV)\"\n  1389→    format: |\n  1390→      CLAIM:    \"[Criterion description] is met/not met in Variant X\"\n  1391→      EVIDENCE: \"[Direct quote or section reference from the variant]\"\n  1392→                OR \"No evidence found — searched sections [list]\"\n  1393→      VERDICT:  MET (1 point) | NOT MET (0 points)\n  1394→    rules:\n  1395→      - \"No partial credit: each criterion is 1 (MET) or 0 (NOT MET)\"\n  1396→      - \"If evaluator cannot cite specific evidence for MET → defaults to NOT MET\"\n  1397→      - \"This prevents hallucinated quality assessments\"\n  1398→      - \"Every MET verdict MUST include a specific evidence citation\"\n  1399→\n  1400→  formula: \"qual_score = total_criteria_met / depth comparison\"\n   652→      severity:\n   653→        Low: \"Depth differs by 1 level\"\n   654→        Medium: \"Depth differs by 2+ levels\"\n   655→        High: \"One variant uses flat level per variant\"\n   660→      severity:\n   661→        Low: \"Minor differences in subsection count\"\n   662→        Medium: \"Significant differences in section granularity\"\n   663→        High: \"Fundamentally different document organization\"\n   664→\n   665→  output_format:\n   666→    id_scheme: \"S-NNN (sequential, starting at S-001)\"\n   667→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Area\", \"Variant A\", \"Variant B\", \"...\", \"Severity\"]\n   668→    scaling: \"For >2 variants, expand table horizontally with one column per variant\"\n   669→```\n   670→\n   671→### Content Diff Engine\n   672→\n   673→Compare approaches topic-by-topic across variants:\n   674→\n   675→```yaml\n   676→content_diff:\n   677→  topic_extraction:\n   678→    process:\n   679→      - \"For each variant, extract topics from H2/H3 section headings\"\n   680→      - \"Build topic inventory: {topic_name, variant_id, section_ref, content_summary}\"\n   681→      - \"Match topics across variants using fuzzy name matching (≥60% word overlap)\"\n   682→    categorize:\n   683→      shared_topics: \"Topics present in 2+ variants\"\n   684→      variant_only_topics: \"Topics in exactly one variant (→ feeds unique contributions)\"\n   685→\n   686→  approach_comparison:\n   687→    for_shared_topics:\n   688→      action: \"For each matched topic, compare how variants address it\"\n   689→      dimensions:\n   690→        - \"Coverage depth: how thoroughly the topic is addressed\"\n   691→        - \"Approach: what strategy or method is proposed\"\n   692→        - \"Detail level: specificity of recommendations or requirements\"\n   693→        - \"Emphasis: what aspects are prioritized\"\n   694→      output: \"Per-topic comparison with approach summaries per variant\"\n   695→\n   696→    coverage_gaps:\n   697→      action: \"Identify topics covered by some variants but not others\"\n   698→      output: \"Gap matrix showing which topics are missing from which variants\"\n   699→\n   700→  severity_assignment:\n   701→    Low: \"Same conclusion via different wording or emphasis\"\n   702→    Medium: \"Materially different approaches to the same topic\"\n   703→    High: \"Fundamentally incompatible strategies for the same topic\"\n   704→\n   705→  output_format:\n   706→    id_scheme: \"C-NNN (sequential, starting at C-001)\"\n   707→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Topic\", \"Variant A Approach\", \"Variant B Approach\", \"...\", \"Severity\"]\n   708→```\n   709→\n   710→### Contradiction Detection Protocol\n   711→\n   712→Structured scan for contradictions across and within variants:\n   713→\n   714→```yaml\n   715→contradiction_detection:\n   716→  claim_extraction:\n   717→    process:\n   718→      - \"Scan each variant for specific, falsifiable statements\"\n   719→      - \"A claim is falsifiable if it can be definitively proven true or false\"\n   720→      - \"EXCLUDE vague statements: 'as appropriate', 'as needed', 'best practices' (without citation)\"\n   721→      - \"EXCLUDE subjective preferences without criteria\"\n   722→    claim_types:\n   723→      factual: \"Assertions about facts, numbers, dates, technologies\"\n   724→      requirement: \"Statements of what must/shall/should be done\"\n   725→      constraint: \"Limitations, thresholds, boundaries\"\n   726→      dependency: \"Sequential requirements, prerequisites, timelines\"\n   727→\n   728→  contradiction_categories:\n   729→    opposing_claims:\n   730→      description: \"Two statements assert opposite or incompatible things about the same subject\"\n   731→      detection: \"For each claim, search other claims for negation or incompatible assertion about the same entity\"\n   732→      example: \"Variant A: 'Use PostgreSQL' vs Variant B: 'Use MongoDB for the same data store'\"\n   733→      scope: \"Both cross-variant and intra-variant (within same document)\"\n   734→\n   735→    requirement_constraint_conflicts:\n   736→      description: \"A stated requirement conflicts with a stated constraint\"\n   737→      detection: \"Match requirement statements against constraint statements; flag where satisfying one violates the other\"\n   738→      example: \"'Must support 10K concurrent users' vs 'Single-threaded architecture required'\"\n   739→\n   740→    impossible_sequences:\n   741→      description: \"Timeline or dependency creates an impossible execution order\"\n   742→      detection: \"Build dependency graph from stated prerequisites; detect cycles or impossible orderings\"\n   743→      example: \"'Module A depends on Module B' AND 'Module B depends on Module A'\"\n   744→\n   745→  severity_assignment:\n   746→    Low: \"Minor wording conflict, does not affect implementation\"\n   747→    Medium: \"Substantive disagreement that affects design decisions\"\n   748→    High: \"Fundamental incompatibility that must be resolved before merge\"\n   749→\n   750→  output_format:\n   751→    id_scheme: \"X-NNN (sequential, starting at X-001)\"\n   752→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Point of Conflict\", \"Variant A Position\", \"Variant B Position\", \"...\", \"Impact\"]\n   753→    evidence_requirement: \"Each contradiction must cite specific text from both variants\"\n   754→```\n   755→\n   756→### Unique Contribution Extraction\n   757→\n   758→Identify ideas present in only one variant:\n   759→\n   760→```yaml\n   761→unique_contribution_extraction:\n   762→  detection:\n   763→    process:\n   764→      - \"For each section/idea in each variant, check if ANY other variant covers the same topic\"\n   765→      - \"Use topic matching from content diff engine (≥60% word overlap = covered)\"\n   766→      - \"Ideas with NO match in any other variant = unique contribution\"\n   767→    granularity: \"Section-level (H2 or H3) — not individual sentences\"\n   768→    exclusions:\n   769→      - \"Boilerplate sections common to the artifact type (table of contents, metadata)\"\n   770→      - \"Minor formatting or stylistic choices\"\n   771→      - \"Restatements of input requirements (not original contributions)\"\n   772→\n   773→  value_assessment:\n   774→    High: \"Addresses a gap no other variant covers; high impact on completeness or correctness\"\n   775→    Medium: \"Useful addition that improves quality but is not critical\"\n   776→    Low: \"Nice to have; minimal impact on overall artifact quality\"\n   777→\n   778→  assessment_criteria:\n   779→    - \"Does this address a requirement not covered elsewhere?\"\n   780→    - \"Does this add risk mitigation not present in other variants?\"\n   781→    - \"Does this improve clarity or usability significantly?\"\n   782→    - \"Is this a novel approach that could strengthen the merged output?\"\n   783→\n   784→  output_format:\n   785→    id_scheme: \"U-NNN (sequential, starting at U-001)\"\n   786→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Variant\", \"Contribution\", \"Value Assessment\"]\n   787→```\n   788→\n   789→### Shared Assumption Extraction Engine (AD-2)\n   790→\n   791→Addresses the \"agreement = no scrutiny\" blind spot by extracting implicit shared assumptions from variant agreement points:\n   792→\n   793→```yaml\n   794→shared_assumption_extraction:\n   795→  purpose: \"Surface hidden preconditions that all variants assume without stating (AD-2)\"\n   796→  integration_point: \"Runs after unique_contribution_extraction, before diff-analysis.md assembly\"\n   797→\n   798→  agreement_identification:\n   799→    action: \"Scan all diff categories (S-NNN, C-NNN, X-NNN, U-NNN) for convergence\"\n   800→    convergence_definition: |\n   801→      An agreement point exists when:\n   802→      - All variants take the same approach to a topic (content diff)\n   803→      - All variants use the same structure for a concept (structural diff)\n   804→      - No contradiction exists between variants on a subject (absence from X-NNN)\n   805→    output: \"List of agreement points with category and description\"\n   806→\n   807→  assumption_enumeration:\n   808→    action: \"For each agreement point, enumerate underlying preconditions\"\n   809→    technique: |\n   810→      Ask: 'What must be true for this agreement to be valid?'\n   811→      Categories of assumptions:\n   812→      - Domain assumptions: 'The system uses X architecture'\n   813→      - Scale assumptions: 'Traffic will not exceed Y'\n   814→      - Dependency assumptions: 'Library Z will be available'\n   815→      - Business assumptions: 'Requirement R is correctly understood'\n   816→      - Technical assumptions: 'Pattern P is applicable here'\n   817→    output: \"List of preconditions per agreement point\"\n   818→\n   819→  classification:\n   820→    STATED: \"Precondition is explicitly mentioned in at least one variant\"\n   821→    UNSTATED: \"Precondition is implicit — no variant states it, but all depend on it\"\n   822→    CONTRADICTED: \"Precondition is inconsistent — variants depend on it but evidence contradicts it\"\n   823→\n   824→  promotion_to_diff_points:\n   825→    condition: \"classification == UNSTATED\"\n   826→    action: \"Promote to synthetic [SHARED-ASSUMPTION] diff point\"\n   827→    id_scheme: \"A-NNN (sequential, starting at A-001 per debate session)\"\n   828→    tag: \"[SHARED-ASSUMPTION]\"\n   829→    inclusion: \"Added to diff-analysis.md Shared Assumptions section\"\n   830→    convergence_impact: \"A-NNN points are included in total_diff_points denominator\"\n   831→\n   832→  output_format:\n   833→    id_scheme: \"A-NNN (sequential, starting at A-001)\"\n   834→    table_columns: [\"#\", \"Agreement Source\", \"Assumption\", \"Classification\", \"Promoted\"]\n   835→    promoted_table_columns: [\"#\", \"Assumption\", \"Impact\", \"Status\"]\n   836→\n   837→  AC_AD2_1:\n   838→    test: \"3 variants assuming 1:1 event-widget mapping\"\n   839→    expected: \"UNSTATED precondition '1:1 event-widget mapping' is surfaced\"\n   840→    verdict: \"A-001 created with classification UNSTATED\"\n   841→```\n   842→\n   843→### diff-analysis.md Artifact Assembly\n   844→\n   845→Combine all diff components into the final Step 1 artifact:\n   846→\n   847→```yaml\n   848→diff_analysis_assembly:\n   849→  output_path: \"<output-dir>/adversarial/diff-analysis.md\"\n   850→\n   851→  assembly_order:\n   852→    1_metadata:\n   853→      content: |\n   854→        # {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_018vet6EmNZSVkPFRNHnrDwC","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018pQh9dJnTee4UN9R3499J1","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/skills/sc-adversarial-protocol/SKILL.md","old_string":"<!-- Phase Execution Engine (M4) will extend this section with:\n     - Phase Executor (D-0020): translates phase config to Mode A/B invocation\n     - Artifact Routing (D-0021): resolves merged_output and all_variants paths\n     - Parallel Phase Scheduler (D-0022): concurrent execution up to --pipeline-parallel N\n     - Pipeline Manifest (D-0023): pipeline-manifest.yaml with return contract\n     - Pipeline Resume (D-0024): --pipeline-resume from manifest\n     - Blind Evaluation (D-0025): --blind metadata stripping\n     - Plateau Detection (D-0026): --auto-stop-plateau\n     - Error Policies (D-0027): halt-on-failure and --pipeline-on-error continue -->","new_string":"### Phase Execution Engine (M4)\n\nPhase Executor, artifact routing, parallel scheduling, pipeline manifest, resume, blind evaluation, plateau detection, and error policies.\n\n#### Phase Executor (D-0020)\n\nTranslates each phase configuration into a Mode A (compare) or Mode B (generate) invocation, scoped to an isolated phase output directory.\n\n```yaml\nphase_executor:\n  purpose: \"Core runtime: translate phase config to Mode A/B invocation with output isolation\"\n\n  translation_rules:\n    generate_phase:\n      maps_to: \"Mode B invocation\"\n      parameters:\n        source: \"Inherited from --source or upstream phase merged_output\"\n        generate: \"Inherited from pipeline config or phase-level override\"\n        agents: \"From phase definition agents field\"\n        depth: \"From phase config.depth or global --depth\"\n        convergence: \"From phase config.convergence or global --convergence\"\n      invocation: \"--source <input> --generate <type> --agents <agent_list>\"\n\n    compare_phase:\n      maps_to: \"Mode A invocation\"\n      parameters:\n        compare: \"All variant outputs from dependency phases (resolved by artifact routing)\"\n        depth: \"From phase config.depth or global --depth\"\n        convergence: \"From phase config.convergence or global --convergence\"\n      invocation: \"--compare <variant_1>,<variant_2>[,...<variant_N>]\"\n\n  output_isolation:\n    directory_structure: \"<pipeline_output>/<phase_id>/\"\n    creation: \"Directory created before phase execution begins\"\n    scoping: \"All phase artifacts written to isolated directory\"\n    example: |\n      pipeline_output/\n        phase_1/\n          variant-opus-architect.md\n          variant-haiku-architect.md\n          diff-analysis.md\n          debate-transcript.md\n          merged-output.md\n        phase_2/\n          variant-opus-security.md\n          variant-haiku-security.md\n          ...\n        phase_3/\n          merged-output.md\n\n  execution_flow:\n    step_1: \"Read phase config (type, agents, dependencies, overrides)\"\n    step_2: \"Resolve input artifacts from dependency phases via artifact routing\"\n    step_3: \"Create isolated output directory: <pipeline_output>/<phase_id>/\"\n    step_4: \"Translate phase type to Mode A or Mode B parameters\"\n    step_5: \"Execute Mode A/B with phase-specific parameters and output directory\"\n    step_6: \"Collect return contract from Mode A/B execution\"\n    step_7: \"Update pipeline manifest with phase results\"\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: '--pipeline \"generate:opus:architect\" produces identical output to direct Mode B'\n    verification: \"Single-phase pipeline output matches direct Mode B invocation\"\n```\n\n#### Artifact Routing (D-0021)\n\nResolves `merged_output` and `all_variants` path references between dependent phases and passes resolved paths as inputs to consuming phases.\n\n```yaml\nartifact_routing:\n  purpose: \"Inter-phase data flow: resolve artifact paths from upstream phases\"\n\n  artifact_types:\n    merged_output:\n      produced_by: [\"generate\", \"compare\"]\n      path_pattern: \"<pipeline_output>/<phase_id>/merged-output.md\"\n      description: \"Final merged artifact from a phase's Mode A/B execution\"\n\n    all_variants:\n      produced_by: [\"generate\"]\n      path_pattern: \"<pipeline_output>/<phase_id>/variant-*.md\"\n      description: \"All variant files produced by a generate phase\"\n\n  path_resolution:\n    algorithm: |\n      Given: consuming_phase, dependency_phase_id, artifact_type\n      1. Compute base_path = <pipeline_output>/<dependency_phase_id>/\n      2. If artifact_type == \"merged_output\":\n         return base_path / \"merged-output.md\"\n      3. If artifact_type == \"all_variants\":\n         return glob(base_path / \"variant-*.md\")\n\n  routing_logic:\n    before_execution: |\n      For each phase about to execute:\n      1. Identify all depends_on phase IDs\n      2. For each dependency:\n         a. Resolve merged_output path\n         b. Verify file exists (error if missing)\n      3. For compare phases:\n         a. Collect all_variants from all dependency phases\n         b. Verify >= 2 variant files total\n      4. Inject resolved paths as input parameters to Mode A/B invocation\n\n  routing_contract:\n    generate_produces: \"merged_output + all_variants\"\n    compare_produces: \"merged_output\"\n    compare_consumes: \"all_variants from dependencies (>= 2 required)\"\n\n  error_handling:\n    missing_artifact: \"STOP: 'Missing dependency artifact: <phase_id>/<artifact_type> not found at <path>'\"\n    insufficient_variants: \"STOP: 'Compare phase <id> requires >= 2 variant inputs, found <N>'\"\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: \"2-phase pipeline (generate -> compare) passes phase 1 merged output as phase 2 variant input\"\n    verification: \"Artifact paths correctly resolved and passed between phases\"\n```\n\n#### Parallel Phase Scheduler (D-0022)\n\nPhases at the same dependency level execute concurrently up to `--pipeline-parallel N` limit, using topological sort for execution ordering.\n\n```yaml\nparallel_phase_scheduler:\n  purpose: \"Concurrent phase execution with dependency-respecting topological order\"\n\n  concurrency_model:\n    grouping: \"Phases at the same DAG level execute in parallel\"\n    limit: \"--pipeline-parallel N caps concurrent phase count (default N=3)\"\n    synchronization: \"Wait for all phases in a level to complete before advancing to next level\"\n    overflow: \"If level has more phases than N, execute in batches of N\"\n\n  algorithm:\n    step_1: \"Receive DAG with assigned levels from dag_builder\"\n    step_2: \"Group phases by level: level_0 = [phases...], level_1 = [phases...], ...\"\n    step_3: \"For each level (ascending order):\"\n    step_3a: \"  Launch phases concurrently (up to --pipeline-parallel N)\"\n    step_3b: \"  Wait for all phases in batch to complete\"\n    step_3c: \"  If any phase failed: apply error policy (see Error Policies)\"\n    step_3d: \"  Run artifact routing for completed phases\"\n    step_4: \"Advance to next level\"\n    step_5: \"After all levels complete: finalize pipeline manifest\"\n\n  topological_guarantee: |\n    Dependent phases NEVER execute before their dependencies complete.\n    This is enforced by level assignment: a phase at level N only depends\n    on phases at levels 0..N-1, which are all completed before level N begins.\n\n  configuration:\n    flag: \"--pipeline-parallel\"\n    default: 3\n    range: \"1-10\"\n    type: \"integer\"\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: \"2-phase parallel generate -> compare produces correct artifacts with no race conditions\"\n    verification: \"Execution order respects dependencies; artifact routing correct\"\n```\n\n#### Pipeline Manifest (D-0023)\n\nTracks execution state: created at pipeline start, updated after each phase with return contract values and checksums.\n\n```yaml\npipeline_manifest:\n  purpose: \"Execution state tracking for resume, audit, and convergence monitoring\"\n  filename: \"pipeline-manifest.yaml\"\n  location: \"<pipeline_output>/pipeline-manifest.yaml\"\n\n  schema:\n    pipeline_id: \"UUID generated at pipeline start\"\n    created_at: \"ISO 8601 timestamp\"\n    pipeline_definition: \"Original --pipeline value (inline or @path)\"\n    global_config:\n      depth: \"string\"\n      convergence_threshold: \"number\"\n      parallel_limit: \"number\"\n      blind_mode: \"boolean\"\n      auto_stop_plateau: \"boolean\"\n      error_policy: \"halt-on-failure | continue\"\n\n    phases:\n      - id: \"Phase ID\"\n        type: \"generate | compare\"\n        agents: [\"agent_spec_1\", ...]\n        level: \"integer (DAG level)\"\n        status: \"pending | running | completed | failed | skipped\"\n        started_at: \"ISO 8601 timestamp | null\"\n        finished_at: \"ISO 8601 timestamp | null\"\n        return_contract:\n          status: \"success | partial | failed\"\n          convergence_score: \"number | null\"\n          merged_output_path: \"string | null\"\n          base_variant: \"string | null\"\n          unresolved_conflicts: \"integer\"\n        artifact_checksums:\n          merged_output: \"SHA-256 | null\"\n          variants: [\"SHA-256\", ...]\n\n  lifecycle:\n    creation: |\n      At pipeline start:\n      1. Generate pipeline_id (UUID)\n      2. Record global_config\n      3. List all phases with status: pending\n      4. Write pipeline-manifest.yaml\n\n    per_phase_update: |\n      After each phase completes:\n      1. Set phase status to completed or failed\n      2. Record return_contract values from Mode A/B execution\n      3. Compute SHA-256 checksums of artifact files\n      4. Record started_at and finished_at timestamps\n      5. Write updated pipeline-manifest.yaml\n\n    finalization: |\n      After pipeline completes:\n      1. Record overall pipeline status\n      2. Ensure all phases have terminal status\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: \"After 3-phase execution, manifest contains all phase results, statuses, and convergence scores\"\n    verification: \"YAML-parseable manifest with per-phase detail\"\n```\n\n#### Pipeline Resume (D-0024)\n\n`--pipeline-resume` reads the manifest, validates artifact checksums, and re-executes from the first incomplete phase.\n\n```yaml\npipeline_resume:\n  purpose: \"Avoid redundant re-execution of completed phases\"\n  trigger: \"--pipeline-resume flag present (in combination with --pipeline)\"\n\n  algorithm:\n    step_1: \"Read pipeline-manifest.yaml from <pipeline_output>/\"\n    step_2: \"For each phase with status: completed\"\n    step_2a: \"  Compute current SHA-256 of artifact files\"\n    step_2b: \"  Compare against manifest artifact_checksums\"\n    step_2c: \"  If match: mark phase as validated (skip re-execution)\"\n    step_2d: \"  If mismatch: mark phase as invalidated\"\n    step_3: \"First invalidated or incomplete phase = resume point\"\n    step_4: \"All phases from resume point onward: re-execute\"\n    step_5: \"All downstream phases of invalidated phases: also re-execute\"\n\n  checksum_validation:\n    scope: \"Artifact files only (merged-output.md, variant-*.md)\"\n    algorithm: \"SHA-256\"\n    mismatch_behavior: \"Re-execute the affected phase AND all downstream dependents\"\n\n  error_handling:\n    missing_manifest: \"STOP: 'No pipeline manifest found at <pipeline_output>/pipeline-manifest.yaml'\"\n    corrupt_manifest: \"STOP: 'Pipeline manifest is malformed: <parse_error>'\"\n    checksum_file_missing: \"Treat as invalidated (re-execute phase)\"\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: \"Resume from phase 2 of 3: phase 1 skipped (checksum valid), phases 2-3 re-execute\"\n    verification: \"Phase 1 artifacts untouched; phases 2-3 produce fresh output\"\n```\n\n#### Blind Evaluation (D-0025)\n\n`--blind` strips model-name metadata from artifacts before compare phases receive variants, ensuring unbiased evaluation (SC-003).\n\n```yaml\nblind_evaluation:\n  purpose: \"Unbiased variant comparison by removing model identification\"\n  trigger: \"--blind flag present\"\n  scope: \"Applied only to artifacts routed to compare phases\"\n\n  stripping_rules:\n    model_names:\n      patterns: [\"opus\", \"haiku\", \"sonnet\", \"claude\", \"gpt\", \"gemini\"]\n      replacement: \"variant-{N}\"\n      case_insensitive: true\n\n    file_content:\n      scan_targets:\n        - \"Model name references in prose text\"\n        - \"Attribution comments (e.g., '<!-- Generated by opus -->')\"\n        - \"Metadata headers containing model identifiers\"\n      action: \"Replace with anonymized identifier (variant-A, variant-B, ...)\"\n\n    file_names:\n      original: \"variant-opus-architect.md\"\n      anonymized: \"variant-A.md\"\n\n  integration_point: |\n    Applied during artifact routing (D-0021), BEFORE passing artifacts to compare phases:\n    1. Copy original artifacts to blind/ subdirectory\n    2. Apply stripping rules to copies\n    3. Pass anonymized copies to compare phase\n    4. Original artifacts preserved in phase output directory\n\n  inactive_behavior: |\n    Without --blind flag: artifacts passed to compare phases with full metadata.\n    Model names and attribution preserved.\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: \"SC-003: merged output after --blind pipeline contains zero model-name references\"\n    verification: \"grep for known model names in merged output returns 0 matches\"\n```\n\n#### Convergence Plateau Detection (D-0026)\n\n`--auto-stop-plateau` halts pipeline execution when convergence delta <5% for 2 consecutive compare phases.\n\n```yaml\nconvergence_plateau_detection:\n  purpose: \"Prevent wasteful additional rounds when convergence has plateaued\"\n  trigger: \"--auto-stop-plateau flag present\"\n\n  algorithm:\n    tracking: \"After each compare phase, record convergence_score in manifest\"\n    delta_calculation: \"delta = abs(current_score - previous_score)\"\n    threshold: 0.05\n    consecutive_required: 2\n    check_point: \"After each compare phase (skip for generate phases)\"\n\n    detection_logic: |\n      After compare phase N completes:\n      1. Read convergence_score from return contract\n      2. If N >= 2 (at least 2 compare phases completed):\n         a. Compute delta = abs(score_N - score_{N-1})\n         b. If delta < 0.05:\n            - Increment consecutive_below_count\n         c. Else:\n            - Reset consecutive_below_count = 0\n      3. If consecutive_below_count >= 2:\n         a. Issue warning: \"Convergence plateau detected\"\n         b. Update manifest with plateau_detected: true\n         c. Halt pipeline (do not execute remaining phases)\n         d. Mark remaining phases as status: skipped\n\n  warning_format: |\n    WARNING: Convergence plateau detected after phase <phase_id>\n      Current delta: <delta> (threshold: 5%)\n      Consecutive below-threshold comparisons: <count>\n      Recommendation: Review debate quality or consider different agent configurations\n      Pipeline halted. Remaining phases skipped.\n\n  inactive_behavior: |\n    Without --auto-stop-plateau: pipeline runs all phases regardless of convergence delta.\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario: \"SC-004: synthetic 3-phase pipeline with plateau triggers warning and halt on phase 3\"\n    verification: \"Warning issued; phases after detection skipped; manifest records plateau_detected\"\n```\n\n#### Error Policies (D-0027)\n\nPipeline error handling: halt-on-failure (default) marks dependents as skipped; `--pipeline-on-error continue` leaves parallel branches running.\n\n```yaml\nerror_policies:\n  purpose: \"Configurable pipeline behavior when a phase fails\"\n\n  policies:\n    halt_on_failure:\n      trigger: \"Default behavior (no flag required)\"\n      behavior: |\n        When a phase fails:\n        1. Mark the failed phase as status: failed in manifest\n        2. Identify all phases that depend on the failed phase (direct + transitive)\n        3. Mark all dependent phases as status: skipped in manifest\n        4. If in parallel execution: cancel sibling phases in the same level\n        5. Halt pipeline execution\n        6. Report failure with skipped phase list\n\n    continue_on_failure:\n      trigger: \"--pipeline-on-error continue\"\n      behavior: |\n        When a phase fails:\n        1. Mark the failed phase as status: failed in manifest\n        2. Identify all phases that depend on the failed phase\n        3. Mark dependent phases as status: skipped in manifest\n        4. BUT: allow independent branches (no dependency on failed phase) to continue\n        5. Continue executing remaining levels\n        6. Report partial completion with failure and skip details\n\n  minimum_variant_constraint:\n    scope: \"Compare phases only\"\n    requirement: \">=2 variant inputs required for meaningful comparison\"\n    check_timing: \"Before compare phase execution begins\"\n    failure_behavior: |\n      If compare phase has <2 variant inputs after artifact routing:\n      1. Mark compare phase as status: failed\n      2. Error: \"Compare phase <id> requires >= 2 variant inputs, found <N>\"\n      3. Apply active error policy (halt or continue)\n\n  configuration:\n    flag: \"--pipeline-on-error\"\n    values: [\"halt\", \"continue\"]\n    default: \"halt\"\n\n  acceptance_test:\n    scenario_halt: \"Failed phase marks dependents as skipped; pipeline stops\"\n    scenario_continue: \"Failed phase marks dependents as skipped; parallel branches continue\"\n    verification: \"Manifest accurately reflects phase statuses under both policies\"\n```"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1818,"cache_read_input_tokens":117344,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1818},"output_tokens":24,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"69de7787-b266-4df7-b3e2-5105646aaa4f","uuid":"7b1d078b-20ec-44d3-b2d8-2a6c704a0d7b"}
